src: "The member of my staff - whose identity, personal information, and safety have been compromised as a result of these fraudulent and false allegations - was in no way responsible for the leak of this information.
mt: “我的工作人员并非此次信息泄露的责任方——其身份、个人信息及人身安全均因这些虚假不实指控而遭到侵害。”

src: This unfounded allegation is completely false and an absolute lie."
mt: 这一无端指控纯属子虚乌有，完全是谎言。

src: Waters' statement quickly drew criticism online, including from former White House press secretary Ari Fleischer.
mt: 沃特斯声明迅速引发网络批评，其中包括前白宫新闻秘书阿里·费莱舍。

src: "This denial is angry," Fleischer wrote.
mt: “这份否认信充满愤怒，”弗莱舍写道。

src: "This suggests she doesn't have the temperment to be a Member of Congress.
mt: 这表明她缺乏成为国会成员所需的性格。

src: When someone is accused of something they didn't do, they must not be angry.
mt: 当一个人被指控做了一件他并未做的事时，他不应该生气。

src: They must not be defiant.
mt: 他们绝不能顶撞。

src: They must not question the motives of the accuser.
mt: 他们不得质疑控方的动机。

src: They must be calm and serene."
mt: 他们必须保持冷静与平和。

src: Fleischer was appearing to compare Waters' reaction to the Democrats' criticism of Judge Kavanaugh, who was accused by critics of seeming too angry during Thursday's hearing.
mt: 弗莱舍尔似乎正在将沃特斯对民主党人批评卡瓦诺法官的反应，与批评者所指的卡瓦诺在周四听证会上显得过于愤怒的情况进行对比。

src: Omar Navarro, a Republican candidate running to unseat Waters in the midterm elections, also voiced his thoughts on Twitter.
mt: 共和党候选人奥马尔·纳瓦罗在竞选中期选举中试图取代沃特斯，他也在推特上发表了观点。

src: "Big if true," he tweeted.
mt: “如果属实，那就非同小可。”他在推文中写道。

src: In her statement, Waters said her office had alerted "the appropriate authorities and law enforcement entities of these fraudulent claims.
mt: 在声明中，沃特斯表示其办公室已向“有关主管部门和执法机构”通报了这些欺诈性索赔。

src: "We will ensure that the perpetrators will be revealed," she continued, "and that they will be held legally liable for all of their actions that are destructible and dangerous to any and all members of my staff."
mt: “我们将确保将肇事者绳之以法，”她继续说道，“并对所有危及我任何一名工作人员安全及利益的行为承担法律责任。”

src: Johnny English Strikes Again review - underpowered Rowan Atkinson spy spoof
mt: 《憨探再出击》影评——Rowan Atkinson 主演的间谍 spoof 片力有未逮

src: It's traditional now to look for Brexit significances in any new film with a British slant and that does seem applicable to this revival of the Johnny English action-comedy spoof franchise - which started back in 2003 with Johnny English and spluttered back to life in 2011 with Johnny English Reborn.
mt: 如今，人们习惯于从任何带有英国色彩的影片中寻找脱欧的意味，这似乎也适用于《憨探奇案》这一动作喜剧 spoof 系列的重启版——该系列于 2003 年以《憨探奇案》开映，并在 2011 年通过《憨探奇案之重启》重新焕发生机。

src: Will tongue-in-cheek self-satire on the subject of how obviously rubbish we are be the nation's new export opportunity?
mt: 难道对“我们明显一无是处”这一主题的半真半谐自嘲，会成为国家新的出口机遇吗？

src: At any rate, the pop-eyed, rubber-faced incompetent Johnny English has had his licence to cock things up renewed for the second time - that name of his signalling more than anything else that he is a broad comic creation designed for non-English-speaking cinemagoing territories.
mt: 无论如何，那副瞪眼鼓腮、无能透顶的“约翰尼·英格利什”再度获得了制造混乱的执照——这个名字本身就已表明，他不过是为非英语国家观众打造的一名滑稽角色。

src: He is of course the daft secret agent who despite his bizarre pretensions to smoothie glamour has got a little bit of Clouseau, a dash of Mr Bean and a dollop of that chap contributing a single note to the Chariots of Fire theme tune at the London 2012 Olympics opening ceremony.
mt: 他当然是一位糊涂的间谍，尽管他故作光滑时髦，却仍带有一丝克劳佐的滑稽、一抹憨豆先生的憨态，以及伦敦 2012 年奥运会开幕式上那位只演奏了《火战车》主题曲中单音的男士的神韵。

src: He's also originally based on the traveller and international man of mystery Atkinson once played in the now forgotten Barclaycard TV ads, leaving chaos in his wake.
mt: 他原本改编自演员阿特金森在如今已被遗忘的巴克莱卡电视广告中扮演的那位神秘的国际旅行者，所到之处留下一片混乱。

src: There are one or two nice moments in this latest JE outing.
mt: 这次最新的《JE》活动中，有几个令人愉悦的瞬间。

src: I loved Johnny English approaching a helicopter while dressed in a medieval suit of armour and the rotor blades briefly clanging against his helmet.
mt: 我特别喜欢《憨豆特工》中约翰尼·英格利士身着中世纪盔甲走向直升机的场景，旋翼叶片还曾短暂地撞击在他的头盔上。

src: Atkinson's gift for physical comedy is on display, but the humour feels pretty underpowered and weirdly superfluous, especially as the "serious" film brands like 007 and Mission Impossible themselves now confidently offer comedy as an ingredient.
mt: 阿特金森擅长肢体喜剧，但其幽默感显得十分乏力，甚至显得多余，尤其是当《007》和《碟中谍》这类“严肃”影片如今也自信地将喜剧作为元素融入其中时。

src: The humour feels as if it is pitched at kids rather than adults, and for me Johnny English's wacky misadventures aren't as inventive and focused as Atkinson's silent-movie gags in the persona of Bean.
mt: 幽默感似乎更偏向儿童而非成人，对我而言，阿特金森以“比恩”这一角色演绎的无声电影式滑稽桥段，比《约翰尼·英格利什》的荒诞冒险更具创意与专注。

src: The perennially topical premise now is that Great Britain is in serious trouble.
mt: 如今，英国正面临严重危机的这一永不过时的论调再度甚嚣尘上。

src: A cyber-hacker has infiltrated Britain's super-secret web network of spies, revealing the identities of all Britain's agents in the field, to the dismay of the agent on duty - a regrettably small role for Kevin Eldon.
mt: 一名网络黑客入侵了英国秘密间谍网络，揭穿了所有驻外英国特工的真实身份，令当值特工倍感沮丧——而这位特工不过是凯文·埃尔顿这样微不足道的小角色。

src: It's the last straw for a prime minister who is a pompous and embattled figure, already suffering a complete meltdown of political unpopularity: Emma Thompson does her very best with this quasi-Teresa-May character but there's nothing much in the script to work with.
mt: 对于一位本就因极度自负而陷入重重困境、政治声望已彻底崩盘的首相而言，这成了压垮骆驼的最后一根稻草：艾玛·汤普森虽竭力塑造这位类同特蕾莎·梅的角色，但剧本却乏善可陈，让她无从施展。

src: Her intelligence advisers inform her that as every single active spy has been compromised, she will have to bring someone out of retirement.
mt: 她的智囊团告知她，既然所有现役间谍都已暴露，她只能启用一位退休的特工。

src: And that means bumbling Johnny English himself, now employed as a schoolmaster in some posh establishment, but giving off-the-record lessons in how to be an undercover operative: some nice gags here, as English offers a School of Rock-type academy of spying.
mt: 这也意味着笨拙的“约翰尼·英格利希”本人，如今受雇于某所贵族学校担任校长，却私下开设特工培训课程：其中不乏精彩笑料，因为英格利希正打造一所类似《摇滚学校》风格的间谍学院。

src: English is whisked back to Whitehall for an emergency briefing and reunited with his former long-suffering sidekick Bough, played again by Ben Miller.
mt: 英语被紧急带回怀特霍尔，与他的老搭档鲍格（由本·米勒再度饰演）重聚。

src: Bough is now a married man, hitched to a submarine commander, a jolly-hockey-sticks role in which Vicki Pepperdine is a bit wasted.
mt: 如今，鲍格已娶妻生子，与一位潜艇指挥官结为连理，这桩“欢乐曲棍球棍”式的婚姻中，维姬·佩珀黛恩略显屈就。

src: So the Batman and Robin of getting things terribly wrong on Her Majesty's Secret Service are back in action, encountering Olga Kurylenko's beautiful femme fatale Ophelia Bulletova.
mt: 因此，詹姆斯·邦德在《007：大战皇家赌场》中犯下的严重错误如今再次上演，他再次陷入行动，却邂逅了由奥尔加·柯瑞丽奥饰演的魅力致命女性角色——奥菲莉亚·布列托娃。

src: Meanwhile, the prime minister is falling dangerously under the spell of the charismatic tech billionaire who claims he can solve Britain's computer woes: the sinister Jason Volta, played by Jake Lacy.
mt: 与此同时，首相正深陷于这位魅力十足的科技亿万富翁的魅惑之中，此人声称能解决英国的电脑危机：他就是由杰克·莱西饰演的邪恶角色——杰森·沃尔塔。

src: English and Bough begin their odyssey of farcical high-jinks: disguised as waiters, they set fire to a flash French restaurant; they create mayhem smuggling themselves aboard Volta's luxury yacht; and English triggers pure anarchy as he attempts to use a Virtual Reality headset to familiarise himself with the interior of Volta's house.
mt: 英格利希和鲍格开启了荒诞滑稽的冒险之旅：他们乔装成侍者，纵火焚烧了一家时髦的法国餐厅；他们策划混乱，偷偷潜入沃尔塔的豪华游艇；英格利希更试图借助虚拟现实头显熟悉沃尔塔宅邸的内部布局，结果却引发了彻底的混乱。

src: All the stops are certainly pulled out for that last sequence, but as amiable and boisterous as it is, there's quite a bit of kids' TV about the whole thing.
mt: 当然，最后的序列动用了所有手段，但即便它充满亲切与喧闹，其中关于儿童电视节目的元素也实在不少。

src: Pretty moderate stuff.
mt: 还算中规中矩。

src: And as with the other Johnny English films I couldn't help thinking: can't the British film industry give Rowan Atkinson a role that really does justice to his talent?
mt: 与此同时，我忍不住想：英国电影产业难道就不能给罗温·艾金森一个真正能体现他才华的角色吗？

src: Labour denies it is devising a plan for Britons to work a four day week but be paid for five days
mt: 工党否认正在制定让英国人每周工作四天但按五天薪资领取的计划。

src: Jeremy Corbyn's Labour Party is to consider a radical plan which will see Britons working a four day week - but getting paid for five.
mt: 杰里米·科尔宾领导的工党拟推出一项激进计划，让英国人每周工作四天，却获得五天的工资。

src: The party reportedly wants company bosses to pass on savings made through the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution to workers by giving them an extra day off.
mt: 据报道，该党希望公司老板将通过人工智能革命节省下来的成本以多放一天假的形式惠及员工。

src: It would see employees enjoy a three-day weekend - but still take home the same pay.
mt: 这将让员工享受三天连休，同时工资照发。

src: Sources said the idea would 'fit' with the party's economic agenda and plans to tilt the country in favour of workers.
mt: 消息人士称，这一构想与党的经济议程及向工人阶级倾斜的国家战略相契合。

src: Shifting to a four-day week has been endorsed by the Trades Union Congress as a way for workers to take advantage of the changing economy.
mt: 工会大会支持推行四天工作制，以便劳动者抓住经济转型的机遇。

src: A senior Labour Party source told The Sunday Times: 'A policy review is expected to be announced before the end of the year.
mt: 工党内一位高级消息人士告诉《星期日泰晤士报》：“预计将在年底前宣布进行政策审查。”

src: 'It won't happen overnight but a four-day working week is an aspiration that fits in with the party's approach to rebalancing the economy in favour of the worker as well as the party's overall industrial strategy.'
mt: “这不会一蹴而就，但四天工作制是与党推动经济再平衡、以劳动者为重的方针相契合的，也符合党的整体产业战略。”

src: The Labour Party would not be the first to endorse such an idea, with the Green Party pledging a four-day working week during its 2017 general election campaign.
mt: 工党并非首个支持这一理念的政治力量，绿党曾在 2017 年大选期间承诺推行四天工作制。

src: The aspiration is currently not being endorsed by the Labour Party as a whole, however.
mt: 不过，目前这一愿景并未得到工党的全面支持。

src: A Labour Party spokesman said: 'A four-day working week is not party policy and it is not being considered by the party.'
mt: 工党发言人表示：“四天工作制并非我党政策，目前也未在考虑之中。”

src: Shadow Chancellor John McDonnell used last week's Labour conference to flesh out his vision for a socialist revolution in the economy.
mt: 影子财政大臣约翰·麦唐纳利用上周的工党大会，进一步阐述了他对经济社会主义革命的构想。

src: Mr McDonnell said he was determined to claw back power from 'faceless directors' and 'profiteers' at utility firms.
mt: 麦冬恩表示，他决心从“面目模糊的董事”和“唯利是图的商人”手中收回公用事业公司的权力。

src: The shadow chancellor's plans also mean current shareholders in water companies may not get back their entire stake as a Labour government could make 'deductions' on the grounds of perceived wrongdoing.
mt: 影子财政大臣的计划还意味着，水务公司的现有股东可能无法拿回全部投资，因为工党政府可能会以涉嫌不当行为为由进行扣减。

src: He also confirmed plans to put workers on company boards and create Inclusive Ownership Funds to hand 10 per cent of private-sector firms' equity to employees, who stand to pocket annual dividends of up to £500.
mt: 他还确认了将在公司董事会中引入员工，并成立包容性所有权基金，向私营部门员工分配其所在公司 10% 的股权，员工每年有望获得高达 500 英镑的股息。

src: Lindsey Graham, John Kennedy tell "60 Minutes" whether the FBI's investigation of Kavanaugh could change their minds
mt: 林赛·格勒姆和约翰·肯尼迪接受《60 分钟》采访，谈他们是否可能因 FBI 对卡瓦诺的调查而改变立场

src: The FBI investigation into accusations against Judge Brett Kavanaugh has delayed a final vote on his nomination to the Supreme Court by at least a week, and raises the question of whether the bureau's findings could sway any Republican senators into pulling their support.
mt: 针对法官布雷特·卡瓦诺的指控，联邦调查局的调查已使其最高法院提名案的最终投票至少推迟了一周，并引发了人们关于调查局的调查结果是否会动摇共和党参议员的支持这一问题。

src: In an interview airing Sunday, "60 Minutes" correspondent Scott Pelley asked Republicans Sens. John Kennedy and Lindsey Graham whether the FBI could unearth anything that would lead them to change their minds.
mt: 周日播出的《60 分钟》节目中，记者斯科特·佩利询问共和党参议员约翰·肯尼迪和林赛·格勒姆，FBI 是否能查出任何能促使他们改变立场的证据。

src: Kennedy appeared more open than his colleague from South Carolina.
mt: 肯尼迪看起来比他的南卡罗来纳州同僚更为开放。

src: "I mean, of course," said Kennedy.
mt: “我当然这么认为，”肯尼迪说。

src: "I said going into the hearing, I said, I've talked to Judge Kavanaugh.
mt: “我在听证会之前就说，我和卡瓦诺法官谈过。”

src: I called him after this happened, that allegation came out, said, 'Did you do it?'
mt: 这件事发生后，我给他打了电话，问：“是你做的吗？”

src: He was resolute, determined, unequivocal."
mt: 他意志坚定，决心已定，态度明确。

src: Graham's vote, however, appears set in stone.
mt: 然而，格雷厄姆的投票意向似乎已定。

src: "My mind's made up about Brett Kavanaugh and it would take a dynamite accusation," he said.
mt: “我对布雷特·卡瓦诺的立场已定，除非有惊天动地的指控，否则我不会改变。”他说。

src: "Dr. Ford, I don't know what happened, but I know this: Brett denied it vigorously," Graham added, referring to Christine Blasey Ford.
mt: “福特博士，我不知道发生了什么事，但我清楚这一点：布雷特对此进行了强烈否认，”格雷厄姆补充道，他指的是克里斯汀·布拉斯基·福特。

src: "And everybody she names couldn't verify it.
mt: 她提到的任何人都无法核实。

src: It's 36 years old.
mt: 三十六岁。

src: I don't see anything new changing."
mt: 我没看到有什么新变化。

src: What is the Global Citizen Festival and Has it Done Anything to Decrease Poverty?
mt: 全球公民法节是什么？它是否真的有助于减少贫困？

src: This Saturday New York will host the Global Citizen Festival, an annual music event which has a hugely impressive line-up of stars performing and an equally impressive mission; ending world poverty.
mt: 本周六，纽约将举办全球公民法庭音乐节。这是一场年度音乐盛会，不仅拥有阵容庞大的明星嘉宾，更肩负着终结世界贫困的使命。

src: Now in its seventh year, the Global Citizen Festival will see tens of thousands of people flock to Central Park's Great Lawn to not only enjoy acts such as Janet Jackson, Cardi B and Shawn Mendes, but also to raise awareness for the event's true goal of ending extreme poverty by 2030.
mt: 如今已进入第七年的全球公民法庭节，将吸引数万名民众涌向中央公园大草坪，不仅欣赏珍妮·杰克逊、卡迪·B 和肖恩·门德斯等艺人的精彩演出，更旨在提升公众对活动真正目标的认识——即于 2030 年前终结极端贫困。

src: The Global Citizen Festival, which stated in 2012, is an extension of the Global Poverty Project, an international advocacy group hoping to end poverty by increasing the number of people actively fighting against it.
mt: 全球公民法节于 2012 年启动，是“全球减贫项目”的延伸。该国际倡导组织旨在通过增加主动投身减贫事业的人数，终结贫困现象。

src: In order to receive a free ticket for the event (unless you were willing to pay for a VIP ticket), concertgoers had to complete a series of tasks, or "actions" such volunteering, emailing a world leader, making a phone call or any other meaningful ways to help raise awareness of their goal of ending poverty.
mt: 为了免费获得活动入场券（除非愿意支付 VIP 票），音乐会的参与者必须完成一系列任务，即“行动”，例如志愿服务、给世界领导人发邮件、打电话，或以其他方式有意义地助力提高对消除贫困这一目标的认识。

src: But just how successful has Global Citizen been with 12 years left to achieve its goal?
mt: 那么，距离实现目标还有 12 年的“全球公民”组织，究竟取得了多大的成功？

src: Is the idea of rewarding people with a free concert a genuine way to persuade people to demand a call for action, or just another case of so-called "clicktivism" - people feeling like they are making a true difference by signing an online petition or sending a tweet?
mt: 用免费音乐会奖励人们，是真正促使他们要求采取行动的有效方式，还是另一种所谓的“点击主义”——即人们觉得只要在网上签名请愿或发条推文就能做出真正贡献？

src: Since 2011, Global Citizen says it has recorded more than 19 million "actions" from its supporters, pushing for a host of different goals.
mt: 自 2011 年以来，全球公民组织（Global Citizen）记录到其支持者发起的行动已超过 1900 万次，旨在推动多项不同目标。

src: It says that these actions have helped spur world leaders to announce commitments and policies equating to more than $37 billion that is set to affect the lives of more than 2.25 billion people by 2030.
mt: 报告指出，这些举措已促使全球领导人宣布了一系列承诺与政策，总额超过 370 亿美元，预计将在 2030 年前惠及超过 22.5 亿人的生活。

src: In early 2018, the group cited 390 commitments and announcements stemming from its actions, at least $10 billion of which have already been disbursed or fundraised.
mt: 2018 年初，该组织表示共有 390 项承诺和声明源于其行动，其中至少有 100 亿美元已拨付或筹集到位。

src: The group estimates the secured funds have so far made a direct impact on nearly 649 million people across the world.
mt: 该组织估计，目前已筹集的资金直接惠及全球近 6.49 亿人。

src: Some of the key commitments include The Power of Nutrition, a U.K. based partnership of investors and implementers committed to "helping children grow to their full potential," promising to provide Rwanda with $35 million to help end malnutrition in the county after receiving more than 4,700 tweets from Global Citizens.
mt: 其中关键承诺之一为“营养力量”，这是一个由英国投资者与执行方组成的合作伙伴关系，致力于“帮助儿童发挥最大潜能”。该伙伴承诺向卢旺达提供 3500 万美元，以在该国终结营养不良问题。此项承诺是在收到全球公民超过 4700 条推文后作出的。

src: "With support from the UK government, donors, national governments, and Global Citizens just like you, we can make the social injustice of undernutrition a footnote in history," The Power of Nutrition ambassador Tracey Ullman told the crowd during a live concert in London in April 2018.
mt: “在英国政府、捐赠者、各国政府以及像您这样的全球公民的支持下，我们可以让营养不良这一社会不公成为历史注脚。”英国营养力量大使 Tracey Ullman 于 2018 年 4 月在伦敦的一场现场演唱会上向观众如是说道。

src: The group also said that after more than 5,000 actions were taken calling on the U.K. improve nutrition for mothers and children, the government announced funding for a project, the Power of Nutrition, that will reach 5 million women and children with nutrition interventions.
mt: 该组织还表示，在采取了 5000 多次呼吁英国改善母婴营养的行动后，政府宣布将为“营养力量”项目提供资金，该项目将惠及 500 万名妇女和儿童，为他们提供营养干预措施。

src: In response to one of the FAQs on its website asking "what makes you think we can end extreme poverty?"
mt: 针对其网站上常见问题之一：“凭什么认为我们可以终结极端贫困？”

src: Global citizen replied: "It'll be a long and hard path - sometimes we will fall and fail.
mt: 全球公民回复道：“这将是一条漫长而艰难的历程——有时我们会跌倒、会失败。"

src: But, like the great civil rights and anti-apartheid movements before us, we will succeed, because we are more powerful together.
mt: 但正如我们之前的伟大民权运动和反种族隔离运动一样，我们将取得胜利，因为团结起来，我们的力量更为强大。

src: Janet Jackson, the Weeknd, Shawn Mendes, Cardi B, Janelle Monáe are among some of the acts performing at this year's event in New York, which will be hosted by Deborra-Lee Furness and Hugh Jackman.
mt: 珍妮·杰克逊、The Weeknd、肖恩·门德斯、卡迪·B 和杰尼·莫耐等艺人将参加今年在纽约举办的活动，该活动由黛博拉-莉·弗内斯和休·杰克曼主持。

src: US could use Navy for "blockade" to hamper Russian energy exports - Interior Secretary
mt: 美国或可动用海军实施“封锁”，以阻碍俄罗斯能源出口——内政部长称

src: Washington can "if necessary" resort to its Navy to prevent Russian energy hitting the markets, including in the Middle East, US Internal Secretary Ryan Zinke has revealed, as cited by Washington Examiner.
mt: 美国能源部长瑞安·津克透露，华盛顿必要时可动用海军阻止俄罗斯能源进入市场，其中包括中东市场。此消息由《华盛顿评论家报》援引报道。

src: Zinke alleged that Russia's engagement in Syria - notably, where it is operating at the invitation of the legitimate government - is a pretext to explore new energy markets.
mt: 津克声称，俄罗斯在叙利亚的介入——尤其是其在合法政府邀请下进行的行动——不过是其探索新能源市场的借口。

src: "I believe the reason they are in the Middle East is they want to broker energy just like they do in eastern Europe, the southern belly of Europe," he has reportedly said.
mt: “据报道称，他表示：‘我认为他们身处中东的原因，是他们想要像在中欧（欧洲南部腹地）那样促成能源交易。’"

src: And, according to to the official, there are ways and means to tackle it.
mt: 此外，据官员称，应对此问题有多种途径和手段。

src: "The United States has that ability, with our Navy, to make sure the sea lanes are open, and, if necessary, to blockade, to make sure that their energy does not go to market," he said.
mt: 他表示：“美国拥有这种能力，凭借我们的海军确保海上通道畅通，并在必要时实施封锁，以确保其能源无法进入市场。”

src: Zinke was addressing the attendees of the event hosted by the Consumer Energy Alliance, a non-profit group which styles itself as the "voice of the energy consumer" in the US.
mt: 津克正在参加消费者能源联盟举办的活动。该非营利组织自称为美国“能源消费者的代言人”。

src: He went to compare Washington's approaches to dealing with Russia and Iran, noting that they are effectively the same.
mt: 他对比了华盛顿处理与俄罗斯和伊朗关系的策略，指出二者实质上并无二致。

src: "The economic option on Iran and Russia is, more or less, leveraging and replacing fuels," he said, while referring to Russia as a "one trick pony" with an economy dependent on fossil fuels.
mt: “对伊朗和俄罗斯的经济选项，大致就是利用和替代燃料，”他说，同时称俄罗斯为“单面手”，因为其经济依赖化石燃料。

src: The statements come as Trump administration has been on a mission to boost the export of its liquefied natural gas to Europe, replacing Russia, the far cheaper option for European consumers.
mt: 特朗普政府正致力于推动其液化天然气向欧洲出口，以取代俄罗斯这一对欧洲消费者更为廉价的选择。

src: For that effect, the Trump administration officials, including US President Donald Trump himself, try to persuade Germany to pull out of the "inappropriate" Nord Stream 2 pipeline project, which according to Trump, made Berlin Moscow's "captive."
mt: 为此，包括美国唐纳德·特朗普总统在内的特朗普政府官员试图说服德国退出“不当”的“北溪 -2"管道项目；据称，该项目使柏林成为莫斯科的“附庸”。

src: Moscow has repeatedly stressed that the $11 billion Nord Stream 2 pipeline, which is set to double the existing pipeline capacity to 110 billion cubic meters, is a purely economic project.
mt: 莫斯科反复强调，耗资 110 亿美元的“北溪 2 号”管道项目纯属经济性质，该项目建成后将使现有管道运力翻倍，达到每年 1100 亿立方米。

src: The Kremlin argues that Washington's fervent opposition to the project is simply driven by economic reasons and is an example of unfair competition.
mt: 克里姆林宫认为，华盛顿对该项目的强烈反对纯粹是出于经济动机，属于不正当竞争。

src: "I believe we share the view that energy cannot be a tool to exercise pressure and that consumers should be able to choose the suppliers," Russian Energy Minister Aleksandr Novak said following a meeting with US Energy Secretary Rick Perry in Moscow in September.
mt: 俄罗斯能源部长诺瓦克在莫斯科与美国能源部长佩里会晤后表示：“我们认为，能源不应被用作施压的工具，消费者应当有权自主选择供应商。”

src: The US stance has drawn backlash from Germany, which has reaffirmed its commitment to the project.
mt: 美国的立场遭到德国的强烈反对，德国重申了对该项目的承诺。

src: Germany's leading organization for industry, the Federation of German Industries (BDI), has called on the US to stay away from the EU energy policy and the bilateral agreements between Berlin and Moscow.
mt: 德国工商联合会（BDI）呼吁美国不要干涉欧盟能源政策，也不应干预柏林与莫斯科之间的双边协议。

src: "I have a big problem when a third state interferes in our energy supply," Dieter Kempf, head of the Federation of German Industries (BDI) said following a recent meeting between German Chancellor Angela Merkel and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
mt: 德国联邦工业联合会主席迪特·肯普夫在近日德国总理默克尔与俄罗斯总统普京会晤后表示：“当第三国干涉我们的能源供应时，我们面临重大难题。”

src: Elizabeth Warren Will Take "Hard Look" At Running For President in 2020, Massachusetts Senator Says
mt: 马萨诸塞州参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦表示，她将认真考虑在 2020 年竞选总统。

src: Massachusetts Senator Elizabeth Warren said on Saturday she would take a "hard look" at running for president following the midterm elections.
mt: 马萨诸塞州参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦表示，她在中期选举后将认真考虑竞选总统。

src: During a town hall in Holyoke, Massachusetts, Warren confirmed she'd consider running.
mt: 马萨诸塞州霍利奥克市的一场市政厅会议上，沃伦确认她愿意参选。

src: "It's time for women to go to Washington and fix our broken government and that includes a woman at the top," she said, according to The Hill.
mt: 她说：“是时候让女性走进华盛顿，修复我们支离破碎的政府，这包括让一位女性登上最高领导岗位。”据《国会山报》报道。

src: "After November 6, I will take a hard look at running for president."
mt: 11 月 6 日之后，我将认真考虑是否竞选总统。

src: Warren weighed in on President Donald Trump during the town hall, saying he was "taking this county in the wrong direction.
mt: 沃伦在市政厅活动上对唐纳德·特朗普总统表示，他正把该县带向错误的方向。

src: "I am worried down to my bones about what Donald Trump is doing to our democracy," she said.
mt: “我对特朗普对我们民主制度造成的破坏深感忧虑，担忧已深入骨髓，”她说。

src: Warren has been outspoken in her criticism of Trump and his Supreme Court nominee Brett Kavanaugh.
mt: 沃伦一直公开批评特朗普及其最高法院大法官提名人布蕾·卡瓦诺。

src: In a tweet on Friday, Warren said "of course we need an FBI investigation before voting."
mt: 周五，沃伦在推文中表示，“投票前当然需要 FBI 进行调查。”

src: A poll released on Thursday, however, showed a majority of Warren's own constituents do not think she should run in 2020.
mt: 然而，周四公布的一项民意调查显示，大多数沃伦自己的支持者并不认为她应该在 2020 年参选。

src: Fifty-eight percent of "likely" Massachusetts voters said the senator should not run, according to the Suffolk University Political Research Center/Boston Globe poll.
mt: 据波士顿大学政治研究中心/波士顿环球社的民意调查显示，58%的“可能”投票给马萨诸塞州的选民表示不应由参议员参选。

src: Thirty-two percent supported such a run.
mt: 三十二%的人支持这样的行动。

src: The poll showed more support for a run by former Governor Deval Patrick, with 38 percent supporting a potential run and 48 percent against it.
mt: 民意调查显示，更多民众支持前州长德瓦尔·帕特里克再次竞选，其中38%的人支持其潜在参选，48%的人反对。

src: Other high profile Democratic names discussed in regard to a potential 2020 run include former Vice President Joe Biden and Vermont Senator Bernie Sanders.
mt: 其他备受瞩目的民主党潜在 2020 年参选人名單包括前副總統約瑟夫·拜登和佛蒙特州參議員伯尼·桑德斯。

src: Biden said he would decide officially by January, the Associated Press reported.
mt: 据美联社报道，拜登表示他将在一月份做出正式决定。

src: Sarah Palin cites Track Palin's PTSD at Donald Trump rally
mt: 莎拉·佩林在特朗普集会上援引特克·佩林的创伤后应激障碍问题

src: Track Palin, 26, spent a year in Iraq after enlisting on Sept.
mt: 26 岁的帕林应征入伍后在伊拉克服役一年。

src: He was arrested and charged in a domestic violence incident on Monday night
mt: 周一晚间，他因涉嫌家庭暴力被捕并被起诉。

src: "What my own son is going through, what he is going through coming back, I can relate to other families who feel ramifications of PTSD and some of the woundedness that our soldiers do return with," she told the audience at a rally for Donald Trump in Tulsa, Oklahoma.
mt: “我儿子所经历的，以及他归来时承受的一切，我能理解那些受到 PTSD 后遗症影响、并带着战争创伤回归的家属们的感受，”她在俄克拉荷马州塔尔萨为唐纳德·特朗普举行的集会上对听众说道。

src: Palin called his arrest "the elephant in the room" and said of her son and other war veterans, "they come back a bit different, they come back hardened, they come back wondering if there is that respect for what it is that their fellow soldiers and airmen, and every other member of the military, has given to the country."
mt: 帕林将他的被捕称为“房间里的大象”，并表示对于他的儿子和其他退伍军人来说，“他们回来后有些不同，变得更为坚韧，开始思索人们是否真正尊重他们——那些与战友和空军人员以及军队每一位成员共同为国家所奉献的一切。”

src: He was arrested on Monday in Wasilla, Alaska, and charged with domestic violence assault on a female, interfering with a report of domestic violence and possession of a weapon while intoxicated, according to Dan Bennett, a spokesman for the Wasilla Police Department.
mt: 据瓦西拉市警察局发言人丹·本内特表示，他于周一在阿拉斯加州瓦西拉市被捕，被控殴打女性、干扰家庭暴力报案以及酒后持枪三项罪名。

src: 18 states, D.C. support challenge to new asylum policy
mt: 18 个州和华盛顿特区支持挑战新庇护政策

src: Eighteen states and the District of Columbia are supporting a legal challenge to a new U.S. policy that denies asylum to victims fleeing gang or domestic violence.
mt: 十八个州和哥伦比亚特区支持对一项新美国政策发起法律诉讼，该政策拒绝为逃离帮派暴力或家庭暴力受害者提供庇护。

src: Representatives from the 18 states and the district filed a friend-of-the-court brief Friday in Washington to support an asylum-seeker challenging the policy, NBC News reported.
mt: NBC 新闻报道，来自 18 个州和哥伦比亚特区的代表于周五在华盛顿提交了一份支持该庇护申请人的“法庭之友”简报，以挑战这一政策。

src: The full name of the plaintiff in the Grace v. Sessions suit that the American Civil Liberties Union filed in August against the federal policy has not been revealed.
mt: 美国公民自由联盟于 8 月提起的“格蕾丝诉塞申斯”案中，原告的全名尚未公开。

src: She said her partner "and his violent gang member sons," abused her but U.S. officials denied her request for asylum July 20.
mt: 她表示其伴侣及“暴徒儿子”曾虐待她，但美国官员于 7 月 20 日拒绝了她寻求庇护的请求。

src: She was detained in Texas.
mt: 她在得克萨斯州被拘留。

src: The states' attorneys supporting Grace described El Salvador, Honduras and Guatemala, which produce a large number of applicants for U.S. asylum, as nations facing pervasive problems with gangs and domestic violence.
mt: 支持格蕾丝的律师们描述了萨尔瓦多、洪都拉斯和危地马拉这三个向美国提出大量庇护申请的国家的状况，指出这些国家普遍存在帮派活动和家庭暴力问题。

src: The new U.S. asylum policy reversed a 2014 decision by the Board of Immigrant Appeals that allowed undocumented immigrants fleeing domestic violence to apply for asylum.
mt: 美国新的庇护政策推翻了一项 2014 年由移民上诉委员会作出的裁决，该裁决曾允许因家庭暴力而逃离的无证移民申请庇护。

src: District of Columbia Attorney General Karl Racine said in a statement Friday that the new policy "ignores decades of state, federal, and international law."
mt: 哥伦比亚特区总检察长卡尔·拉辛周五在声明中表示，这项新政策“无视了数十年来州、联邦及国际法律的规定”。

src: "Federal law requires that all asylum claims be adjudicated on the particular facts and circumstances of the claim, and such a bar violates that principle," the friend-of-the court brief said.
mt: 法院之友简报指出，联邦法律要求所有的庇护申请均须根据具体事实与情节进行裁决，而此类禁令违反了这一原则。

src: Attorneys further argued in the brief that the policy denying immigrants entry hurts the U.S. economy, saying they are more likely to become entrepreneurs and "supply necessary labor."
mt: 律师在诉状中进一步辩称，限制移民入境的政策损害了美国经济，因为移民更有可能成为企业家并“提供必要的劳动力”。

src: Attorney General Jeff Sessions ordered immigration judges to no longer grant asylum to victims fleeing domestic abuse and gang violence in June.
mt: 6 月，司法部长杰夫·塞申斯下令，移民法官不得再向遭受家庭暴力或帮派暴力的受害者授予庇护。

src: "Asylum is available for those who leave their home country because of persecution or fear on account of race, religion, nationality, or membership in a particular social group or political opinion," Sessions said in his June 11 announcement of the policy.
mt: “赛恩斯在 6 月 11 日发布的该政策声明中表示，庇护面向那些因种族、宗教、国籍、特定社会群体成员身份或政治观点而遭受迫害或恐惧其所在国的人。”

src: Asylum was never meant to alleviate all problems -- even all serious problems -- that people face every day all over the world.
mt: 庇护从来就不是为了解决人们每天在全球各地所面临的所有问题——哪怕是所有严重的问题。

src: Desperate rescue efforts in Palu as death toll doubles in race to find survivors
mt: 帕卢救援工作陷入绝望，死亡人数翻倍，各方争分夺秒寻找幸存者。

src: For survivors, the situation was increasingly dire.
mt: 对于幸存者而言，局势愈发危急。

src: "It feels very tense," said 35-year-old mother Risa Kusuma, comforting her feverish baby boy at an evacuation centre in the gutted city of Palu.
mt: 35 岁的母亲库苏马丽萨（Risa Kusuma）在已被夷为平地的帕卢市疏散中心，一边安抚着发着高烧的儿子，一边说道：“感觉非常紧张。”

src: "Every minute an ambulance brings in bodies.
mt: 每分钟都有救护车送来遗体。

src: Clean water is scarce."
mt: 清洁用水十分匮乏。

src: Residents were seen returning to their destroyed homes, picking through waterlogged belongings, trying to salvage anything they could find.
mt: 居民们回到被毁的家，在湿透的物品中翻找，试图抢救一切还能救的东西。

src: Hundreds of people were injured and hospitals, damaged by the magnitude 7.5 quake, were overwhelmed.
mt: 震级达 7.5 级的地震导致数百人受伤，医院受损不堪，不堪重负。

src: Some of the injured, including Dwi Haris, who suffered a broken back and shoulder, rested outside Palu's Army Hospital, where patients were being treated outdoors due to continuing strong aftershocks.
mt: 包括背和肩膀受伤的德维·哈里斯（Dwi Haris）在内的一些伤者，在帕卢的军医院外休息，因为余震持续强烈，患者需在户外接受治疗。

src: Tears filled his eyes as he recounted feeling the violent earthquake shake the fifth-floor hotel room he shared with his wife and daughter.
mt: 他泪流满面，回想起与妻子和女儿共住的五楼酒店房间在地震中剧烈摇晃的情景。

src: "There was no time to save ourselves.
mt: 已来不及自救。

src: I was squeezed into the ruins of the wall, I think," Haris told Associated Press, adding that his family was in town for a wedding.
mt: “我觉得自己被挤进了墙的废墟里，”哈里斯告诉美联社，并表示他的家人当时是来镇上参加婚礼的。

src: "I heard my wife cry for help, but then silence.
mt: 我听见妻子呼救，随后却是一片死寂。

src: I don't know what happened to her and my child.
mt: 我不知道她和我孩子之间发生了什么事。

src: I hope they are safe."
mt: 希望他们平安无事。

src: U.S. ambassador accuses China of 'bullying' with 'propaganda ads'
mt: 美国驻美大使指责中国以“宣传广告”进行“霸凌”。

src: A week after an official Chinese newspaper ran a four-page ad in a U.S. daily touting the mutual benefits of U.S.-China trade, the U.S. ambassador to China accused Beijing of using the American press to spread propaganda.
mt: 一周前，一家中国官方报纸在美国的一份日报上刊登了一则四版广告，宣扬中美贸易的互利共赢；随后，美国驻中国大使指责北京利用美国媒体进行 propaganda 宣传。

src: U.S. President Donald Trump last Wednesday referred to the China Daily's paid supplement in the Des Moines Register - the state of Iowa's biggest selling newspaper - after accusing China of seeking to meddle in the Nov. 6 U.S. congressional elections, a charge China denies.
mt: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普上周三在《得梅因纪事报》上提及《中国日报》的付费专栏，该报是爱荷华州销量最大的报纸。特朗普指责中国企图干预 11 月 6 日的美国国会选举，而中方对此予以否认。

src: Trump's accusation that Beijing was trying to meddle in U.S. elections marked what U.S. officials told Reuters was a new phase in an escalating campaign by Washington to put pressure on China.
mt: 特朗普指责北京试图干涉美国大选，这被美国官员告诉路透社，标志着华盛顿向中国施压的升级行动进入新阶段。

src: While it is normal for foreign governments to place advertisements to promote trade, Beijing and Washington are currently locked in an escalating trade war that has seen them level rounds of tariffs on each other's imports.
mt: 虽然外国政府发布广告以促进贸易属于正常现象，但北京与华盛顿目前正陷入不断升级的贸易战中，双方已互相对中国和美国进口的商品加征多轮关税。

src: China's retaliatory tariffs early in the trade war were designed to hit exporters in states such as Iowa that supported Trump's Republican Party, Chinese and U.S. experts have said.
mt: 中国专家和美國專家表示，中國在貿易戰初期實施的報復性關稅，旨在打擊支持特朗普共和黨人選的州（如愛荷華州）的出口商。

src: Terry Branstad, the U.S. ambassador to China and the former longtime governor of Iowa, a major exporter of agricultural goods to China, said Beijing had hurt American workers, farmers and businesses.
mt: 美国驻华大使、前爱荷华州长期州长特里·布兰斯塔德表示，北京损害了美国工人、农民和企业的利益。爱荷华州是中国农业产品的主要出口地。

src: China, Branstad wrote in an opinion piece in Sunday's Des Moines Register, "is now doubling down on that bullying by running propaganda ads in our own free press."
mt: 在周日《得梅因纪事报》上发表的评论文章中，布兰斯坦写道：“中国现在通过在我们要的新闻媒体上投放宣传广告，变本加厉地推行这种霸凌行径。”

src: "In disseminating its propaganda, China's government is availing itself of America's cherished tradition of free speech and a free press by placing a paid advertisement in the Des Moines Register," Branstad wrote.
mt: 布兰斯塔德写道：“中国政府在传播其宣传品时，利用了美国所珍视的言论自由和新闻自由传统，在《得梅因纪事报》上刊登了付费广告。”

src: "In contrast, at the newsstand down the street here in Beijing, you will find limited dissenting voices and will not see any true reflection of the disparate opinions that the Chinese people may have on China's troubling economic trajectory, given that media is under the firm thumb of the Chinese Communist Party," he wrote.
mt: 相比之下，在北京街头的报亭里，你只能找到少数持异议的声音，根本看不到中国民众对中国经济困境所持的多元观点的真实反映，因为媒体牢牢掌握在中国共产党的手中。

src: He added that "one of China's most prominent newspapers dodged the offer to publish" his article, although he did not say which newspaper.
mt: 他还表示，中国一家颇具影响力的报纸拒绝刊登他的文章，但他并未透露是哪家报纸。

src: Republicans Alienating Women Voters Ahead of Midterms With Kavanaugh Debacle, Analysts Warn
mt: 在中期选举前夕，分析人士警告称，盖尔诺斯卡班案导致共和党疏远女性选民。

src: As many top Republicans stand-by and defend Supreme Court nominee Brett Kavanaugh in the face of several allegations of sexual assault, analyst have warned they will see a backlash, particularly from women, during the upcoming midterm elections.
mt: 鉴于众多共和党高层在面临多项性侵指控的情况下仍坚持支持最高法院提名人凯文·卡瓦诺，分析人士警告称，在接下来的中期选举中，他们可能会遭遇反弹，尤其是来自女性的反对声浪。

src: The emotions surrounding this have been extremely high, and most Republicans are on record already showing they wanted to go forward with a vote.
mt: 围绕此事的舆论情绪高涨，绝大多数共和党人此前已表态支持推进表决。

src: Those things can't be walked back," Grant Reeher, a professor of political science at Syracuse University's Maxwell School told The Hill for an article published Saturday.
mt: “这些事无法挽回。”锡拉丘兹大学马克斯韦学院的政治学教授格兰特·里尔在周六发表的一篇《国会山报》文章中说道。

src: Reeher said he doubts Senator Jeff Flake's (R-Arizona) last-minute push for an FBI investigation will be enough to placate angry voters.
mt: 里查认为，参议员杰夫·弗莱克（共和党 - 亚利桑那州）临阵要求联邦调查局展开调查，恐不足以平息愤怒的选民。

src: "Women are not going to forget what happened yesterday - they are not going to forget it tomorrow and not in November," Karine Jean-Pierre, a senior adviser and national spokeswoman for the progressive group MoveOn said on Friday, according to the Washington, D.C. newspaper.
mt: 据华盛顿特区一家报纸报道，进步团体 MoveOn 的高级顾问兼全国发言人卡琳·让 - 皮埃尔周五表示：“女性不会忘记昨天发生的事——她们明天也不会忘记，11 月更不会忘记。”

src: On Friday morning, protestors chanted "November is coming!" as they demonstrated in the hallway of the Senate as the Republicans controlling the Judiciary Committee chose to move forward with Kavanaugh's nomination despite the testimony of Dr. Christine Blasey Ford, Mic reported.
mt: 周五上午，抗议者在参议院走廊高呼“十一月就要来了！”，以示对共和党控制的司法委员会不顾克里斯汀·布雷耶·福尔博士证词而推进卡瓦诺提名这一决定的抗议，Mic 报道。

src: "Democratic enthusiasm and motivation is going to be off the chart," Stu Rothenberg, a nonpartisan political analyst, told the news site.
mt: 非党派政治分析师斯图·罗滕伯格在接受该新闻网站采访时表示：“民主热情与动力将空前高涨。”

src: "People are saying it's already been high; that's true.
mt: 人们说它已经很高了，确实如此。

src: But it could be higher, particularly among swing women voters in the suburbs and younger voters, 18- to 29-year-olds, who while they don't like the president, often don't vote."
mt: 但实际支持率可能更高，特别是在郊区摇摆不定的女性选民和 18 至 29 岁的年轻选民中，这些群体虽然不喜欢总统，却往往选择不投票。

src: Even before Ford's public testimony detailing her allegations of sexual assault against the Supreme Court nominee, analysts suggested a backlash could follow if Republicans pushed forward with the confirmation.
mt: 在福特公开作证详述其对最高法院提名人的性侵犯指控之前，分析人士便表示，若共和党继续推进确认程序，可能会引发反弹。

src: "This has become a muddled mess for the GOP," said Michael Steele, former chairman of the Republican National Committee, early last week, according to NBC News.
mt: NBC 报道，共和党全国委员会前主席迈克尔·斯蒂尔（Michael Steele）在上周一表示：“这已成为共和党的混乱局面。”

src: "It's just not about the committee vote or the final vote or whether Kavanaugh is put on the bench, it's also about the way Republicans have handled this and how they have treated her," Guy Cecil, director of Priorities USA, a group that helps to elect Democrats, pointed out to the news channel.
mt: 盖伊·塞西尔，民主党助选组织“优先美国行动”的主任，向新闻频道指出：“这不仅关乎委员会投票、最终投票，也不关乎卡瓦诺是否获任大法官，更关乎共和党人处理此事的方式以及他们对待她的态度。”

src: However, Americans appear to be somewhat split over who to believe in the wake of Ford's and Kavanaugh's testimonies, with slightly more siding with the latter.
mt: 然而，在福特和卡瓦诺作证之后，美国民众似乎对此看法不一，略倾向于后者。

src: A new poll from YouGov shows that 41 percent of respondents definitely or probably believed Ford's testimony, while 35 percent said they definitely or probably believed Kavanaugh.
mt: YouGov 的一项新民意调查显示，41% 的受访者认为福特作证的内容可信或可能可信，而35% 的受访者认为卡瓦诺作证的内容可信或可能可信。

src: Additionally, 38 percent said they thought Kavanaugh has probably or definitely lied during his testimony, while just 30 percent said the same about Ford.
mt: 此外，38%的受访者认为卡瓦诺在作证时可能或肯定说了谎，而只有30%的受访者对福特持相同看法。

src: After the push from Flake, the FBI is currently investigating the allegations brought forward by Ford as well as at least one other accuser, Deborah Ramirez, The Guardian reported.
mt: 在弗莱克施压后，《卫报》报道，联邦调查局目前正在调查福特提出的指控以及至少另一名指控者德博拉·拉米雷斯提出的指控。

src: Ford testified before the Senate Judiciary Committee under oath last week that Kavanaugh drunkenly assaulted her at the age of 17.
mt: 上周，福特在参议院司法委员会作证时宣誓称，卡凡恩在她 17 岁时曾醉酒对其进行性侵犯。

src: Ramirez alleges that the Supreme Court nominee exposed his genitals to her while they attended a party during their time studying at Yale in the 1980s.
mt: 拉米雷斯指控，最高法院候选人提名人在 20 世纪 80 年代于耶鲁大学就读期间，曾在一场聚会上向她暴露生殖器。

src: The Inventor of the World Wide Web Plans to Start a New Internet to Take on Google and Facebook
mt: 万维网发明者计划打造新互联网，挑战谷歌与脸书

src: Tim Berners-Lee, the inventor of the World Wide Web, is launching a startup that seeks to rival Facebook, Amazon and Google.
mt: 万维网发明者蒂姆·伯纳斯-李（Tim Berners-Lee）正在启动一家初创公司，旨在挑战 Facebook、亚马逊和谷歌。

src: The technology legend's latest project, Inrupt, is a company that builds off of Berners-Lee's open source platform Solid.
mt: 技术传奇人物伯纳斯 - 李的最新项目 Inrupt，是一家基于其开源平台 Solid 构建的公司。

src: Solid allows users to choose where their data is stored and what people are allowed to have access to what information.
mt: Solid 允许用户选择数据存储位置，并决定哪些人可以访问其信息。

src: In an exclusive interview with Fast Company, Berners-Lee joked that the intent behind Inrupt is "world domination."
mt: 在《快公司》杂志的独家专访中，伯纳斯 - 李开玩笑说，Inrupt 的初衷是“世界征服”。

src: "We have to do it now," he said of the startup.
mt: “我们必须现在就行动，”他说道。

src: "It's a historical moment."
mt: 这是一个历史性时刻。

src: The app uses Solid's technology to allow people to create their own "personal online data store" or a POD.
mt: 该应用利用 Solid 的技术，允许用户创建属于自己的“个人在线数据存储”（POD）。

src: It can contain contact lists, to-do lists, calendar, music library and other personal and professional tools.
mt: 它可以包含联系人列表、待办事项、日历、音乐库以及其他个人和专业工具。

src: It's like Google Drive, Microsoft Outlook, Slack and Spotify are all available on one browser and all at the same time.
mt: 这就好比 Google Drive、Microsoft Outlook、Slack 和 Spotify 等应用都能在一个浏览器中同时使用。

src: What's unique about the personal online data store is that it is completely up to the user who can access what kind of information.
mt: 个人在线数据存储的独特之处在于，用户可以完全自主决定哪些信息可以被访问。

src: The company calls it "personal empowerment through data."
mt: 公司称之为“通过数据实现个人赋能”。

src: The idea for Inrupt, according to the company's CEO John Bruce, is for the company to bring resources, process and appropriate skills to help make Solid available to everyone.
mt: 根据 Inrupt 首席执行官约翰·布鲁斯的说法，该公司的理念是汇聚资源、流程与专业技能，以助力让 Solid 面向所有人开放。

src: The company currently consists of Berners-Lee, Bruce, a security platform bought by IBM, some on-staff developers contracted to work on the project, and a community of volunteer coders.
mt: 该公司目前由伯纳斯 - 李（Berners-Lee）和布鲁斯（Bruce）领导，成员还包括 IBM 收购的安全平台、部分受雇于公司的开发人员，以及一群志愿程序员组成的社区。

src: Starting this week, technology developers around the world could create their own decentralized apps using the tools available on the Inrupt website.
mt: 从本周起，全球各地的技术开发者可利用 Inrupt 网站上提供的工具，创建属于自己的去中心化应用。

src: Berners-Lee said that he and his team are not talking to "Facebook and Google about whether or not to introduce a complete change where all their business models are completely upended overnight.
mt: 伯纳斯 - 李表示，他和他的团队并未在与"Facebook 和 Google 讨论是否要引入一项彻底的变革，从而一夜之间彻底颠覆它们所有的商业模式。

src: "We are not asking their permission."
mt: 我们并未寻求他们的许可。

src: In a post on Medium published on Saturday, Berners-Lee wrote that Inrupt's "mission is to provide commercial energy and an ecosystem to help protect the integrity and quality of the new web built on Solid."
mt: 周六，伯纳 - 李在 Medium 上发文称，Inrupt 的使命是提供商业动力和生态系统，以保护基于 Solid 构建的新网络的完整性和质量。

src: In 1994, Berners-Lee transformed the Internet when he established the World Wide Web Consortium at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.
mt: 1994 年，伯纳斯 - 李在麻省理工学院创立万维网联盟，推动了互联网的发展。

src: In recent months, Berners-Lee has been an influential voice in the net neutrality debate.
mt: 近年来，伯纳斯 - 李一直是网络中立性辩论中的重要声音。

src: Even while launching Inrupt, Berners-Lee will remain the Founder and Director of World Wide Web Consortium, the Web Foundation and the Open Data Institute.
mt: 即使 Launch Inrupt，伯纳斯·李仍将担任万维网联盟、网络基金会和开放数据研究所的创始人兼总监。

src: "I'm incredibly optimistic for this next era of the web," Berners-Lee added.
mt: 伯纳斯 - 李补充说：“我对互联网的这个新时代充满极度的乐观。”

src: Bernard Vann: WW1 Victoria Cross cleric celebrated
mt: 伯纳德·范：一战获维多利亚十字勋章的神职人员获致敬

src: The only Church of England cleric to win a Victoria Cross during World War One as a combatant has been celebrated in his hometown 100 years on.
mt: 一战期间，唯一作为战斗人员获颁维多利亚十字勋章的英国国教神职人员，如今在其家乡迎来了百年纪念。

src: Lt Col The Reverend Bernard Vann won the award on 29 September 1918 in the attack at Bellenglise and Lehaucourt.
mt: 陆军上校伯纳德·范牧师于 1918 年 9 月 29 日在贝尔朗利斯和勒奥库尔的战斗中获此殊荣。

src: However, he was killed by a sniper four days later and never knew he had won the highest British military honour.
mt: 然而，他却在四天后被狙击手射杀，至死都未曾知晓自己荣获了英国最高的军事荣誉。

src: A commemorative stone was unveiled by his two grandsons at a parade in Rushden, Northamptonshire, on Saturday.
mt: 周六，北安普敦郡拉什登举行游行时，他的两个孙子揭幕了一块纪念石碑。

src: One of his grandsons, Michael Vann, said it was "brilliantly symbolic" the stone would be revealed exactly 100 years on from his grandfather's award-winning feat.
mt: 他的孙子迈克尔·范恩表示，这块石碑恰好在他祖父荣获殊荣一百周年之际揭幕，极具象征意义。

src: According to the London Gazette, on 29 September 1918 Lt Col Vann led his battalion across the Canal de Saint-Quentin "through a very thick fog and under heavy fire from field and machine guns."
mt: 根据《伦敦公报》记载，1918 年 9 月 29 日，万上校率领其旅穿越圣康坦运河，“在浓雾弥漫、并遭受密集的地面及机枪火力压制下”。

src: He later rushed up to the firing line and with the "greatest gallantry" led the line forward before rushing a field-gun single-handed and knocked out three of the detachment.
mt: 随后，他迅速冲至前沿阵地，以“最无畏的英勇”率领队伍向前推进，继而单人突入一门野战炮，一举击毁该部队三门火炮。

src: Lt Col Vann was killed by a German sniper on 4 October 1918 - just over a month before the war ended.
mt: 范中校于 1918 年 10 月 4 日遭德国狙击手击毙，距离战争结束尚有一月有余。

src: Michael Vann, 72, said his grandfather's actions were "something that I know that I could never live up to but something which is humbling."
mt: 72 岁的迈克尔·范表示，祖父的所作所为“是我永远无法企及的，但令人深感谦卑”。

src: He and his brother Dr James Vann also laid a wreath after the parade, which was led by the Brentwood Imperial Youth Band.
mt: 游行结束后，他与弟弟詹姆斯·范恩博士一同献上了花圈，游行队伍由布伦特伍德帝国青年乐队带领。

src: Michael Vann said he was "feeling very honoured to play a part in the parade" and added "the valour of a genuine hero is being demonstrated by the support that is going to be given by a lot of people."
mt: 迈克尔·范表示，他“感到十分荣幸能参与游行”，并补充道，“众多人士将给予的支持正体现出一位真正英雄的英勇精神。”

src: MMA fans stayed up all night to watch Bellator 206, they got Peppa Pig instead
mt: MMA 粉丝熬夜观看 Bellator 206，结果却看到了小猪佩奇。

src: Imagine this, you have stayed up all night to watch the a packed Bellator 206 only to be denied watching the main event.
mt: 试想一下，你通宵达旦只为观看一场座无虚席的 Bellator 206 赛事，结果却无缘目睹主赛对决。

src: The bill from San Jose contained 13 fights, including six on the main card and was being shown live through the night in the UK on Channel 5.
mt: 来自圣何塞的赛事包含 13 场比赛，其中 6 场在头牌赛上，并通过英国第五频道全程直播。

src: At 6am, just as Gegard Mousasi and Rory MacDonald were preparing to face each other, viewers in the UK were left stunned when the coverage changed to Peppa Pig.
mt: 凌晨 6 点，就在 Gegard Mousasi 和 Rory MacDonald 准备对决之际，英国观众看到的直播画面却突然切换成了《佩佩猪》，令人大吃一惊。

src: Some were unimpressed after they had stayed awake until the early hours especially for the fight.
mt: 有些人对此兴致缺缺，尤其是那些为了观看比赛而熬到凌晨的人们。

src: One fan on Twitter described the switch to the children's cartoon as "some sort of sick joke."
mt: 一位推特用户将转向儿童动画的做法形容为“某种恶心的玩笑”。

src: "It's government regulation that at 6 a.m. that content was not suitable so they had to switch to children's programming, " said Dave Schwartz, Bellator senior vice president of marketing and communication, when asked about the transmission.
mt: “是政府规定在早上 6 点该节目内容不适宜，因此他们必须切换到儿童节目，”贝尔莱托高级副总裁兼市场传播总监戴夫·施瓦茨在被问及播出情况时表示。

src: ""Peppa the pig," yes."
mt: “小猪佩奇”，没错。

src: Bellator company president Scott Coker said that they are going to work on their scheduling to include UK viewers in the future.
mt: Bellator 公司总裁斯科特·科克表示，公司将调整赛程安排，未来将纳入英国观众的观看需求。

src: "I think that when I think about the replay, I think that we can probably work it out," Coker said.
mt: “我想，当回想起那场比赛时，我们大概能够解决这个问题的，”科克说。

src: "But it's six in the morning on a Sunday there and we won't be able to work this out until Sunday our time, Monday their time.
mt: “但那边是周日上午六点，我们得等到那边周日（也就是我们周一）才能解决这个问题。”

src: But we are working on it.
mt: 不过我们确实在努力。

src: Believe me, when it switched over there were a lot of texts going back and forth and they all were not friendly.
mt: 相信我，当时转换过程中双方来回发送了大量信息，而且内容都不友好。

src: We were trying to fix it, we thought it was a technical glitch.
mt: 我们正试图修复它，原本以为只是技术故障。

src: But it wasn't, it was a governmental issue.
mt: 但实际上，这是个政府层面的问题。

src: I can promise you the next time it's not going to happen.
mt: 我向你保证，下次这种情况绝不会再发生。

src: We'll keep it down to five fights instead of six - like we normally do - and we tried to overdeliver for the fans and we just went over.
mt: 我们将比赛场次从六场减至五场，一如往常；我们曾试图为观众提供更多精彩表现，结果却超出了预期。

src: It's an unfortunate situation."
mt: 这是一件不幸的事情。

src: Desert Island Discs: Tom Daley felt 'inferior' over sexuality
mt: 孤岛遗言：汤姆·戴利因性取向感到“低人一等”

src: Olympic diver Tom Daley says he grew up feeling inferior to everyone because of his sexuality - but that gave him the motivation to become a success.
mt: 奥运跳水选手汤姆·戴利表示，因自己的性取向，他从小感到自卑，但这反而激励他取得了成功。

src: The 24-year-old said he did not realise until he went to secondary school that "not everyone is like me."
mt: 这位 24 岁的年轻人表示，他直到上中学才发现“并非所有人都像我一样”。

src: Speaking on the first Radio 4 Desert Island Discs presented by Lauren Laverne, he said he spoke out about gay rights to give others "hope."
mt: 在接受劳伦·拉弗恩主持的首期《荒岛唱片》（Radio 4）节目访谈时，他表示自己为争取同性恋权利发声，是为了给予他人“希望”。

src: He also said becoming a parent made him care less about winning the Olympics.
mt: 他还表示，成为父母后，他对赢得奥运会的执念淡了许多。

src: The regular presenter of the long-running show, Kirsty Young, has taken a number of months off because of illness.
mt: 长期主持该节目的基尔斯蒂·杨（Kirsty Young）因生病已休了好几个月。

src: Appearing as a castaway on Laverne's first programme, Daley said he felt "less than" everyone else growing up because "it wasn't socially acceptable to like boys and girls."
mt: 在莱弗恩的首档节目中，道利作为嘉宾登场，他表示自己小时候感到“低人一等”，因为“喜欢男孩和女孩在当时并不被社会所接受”。

src: He said: "To this day, those feelings of feeling less than, and feeling different, have been the real things that have given me the power and strength to be able to succeed."
mt: 他说：“直到今天，那种觉得自己不如他人、与众不同的感觉，才是真正赋予我力量与成功动力的源泉。”

src: He wanted to prove that he was "something," he said, so that he did not disappoint everyone when they eventually found out about his sexuality.
mt: 他想证明自己是个“有本事的人”，因此当大家最终得知他的性取向时，他不想让人失望。

src: The two-time bronze Olympic medallist has become a high-profile LGBT campaigner and used his appearance at this year's Commonwealth Games in Australia to appeal for more countries to decriminalise homosexuality.
mt: 这位两届奥运会铜牌得主已转型为高调的 LGBTQ+ 活动家，并借今年在澳大利亚举行的英联邦运动会之机，呼吁更多国家将同性恋除罪化。

src: He said he spoke out because he felt lucky to be able to live openly without ramifications and wanted to give others "hope."
mt: 他表示自己发声是因为感到幸运，能够毫无顾忌地公开生活，并希望能为他人带来“希望”。

src: The three-time world champion said falling in love with a man - US film-maker Dustin Lance Black, who he met in 2013 - "caught me by surprise."
mt: 这位三届世界冠军表示，2013 年遇到的美国电影人达斯汀·兰斯·布莱克让他坠入爱河“完全出乎意料”。

src: Daley married the Oscar winner, who is 20 years his senior, last year but he said the age gap had never been an issue.
mt: 去年，戴利娶了比他大 20 岁的奥斯卡影后，但他表示年龄差距从未成为问题。

src: "When you go through so much at such a young age" - he went to his first Olympics aged 14 and his father died of cancer three years later - he said that it was hard to find someone the same age who had experienced similar highs and lows.
mt: “在如此年轻的年纪就经历了这么多”——他 14 岁便参加了首届奥运会，三年后父亲因癌症去世——他表示，很难找到同龄人能理解这种大喜大悲的交织经历。

src: The couple became parents in June, to a son called Robert Ray Black-Daley, and Daley said his "whole perspective" had changed.
mt: 这对夫妇今年六月喜得贵子，取名罗伯特·雷·布莱克-戴利，戴利表示自己的“整个观念”都发生了改变。

src: "If you had asked me last year, it was all about 'I need to win a gold medal'," he said.
mt: “如果你去年问我，我只会说要赢得一枚金牌，”他说。

src: "You know what, there are bigger things than Olympic gold medals.
mt: 要知道，有些东西比奥运金牌更重要。

src: My Olympic gold medal is Robbie."
mt: 我的奥运金牌得主是罗比。

src: His son has the same name as his father Robert, who died in 2011 aged 40 after being diagnosed with brain cancer.
mt: 他的儿子与父亲罗伯特同名，罗伯特因脑癌于 2011 年去世，享年 40 岁。

src: Daley said his dad did not accept he was going to die and one of the last things he had asked was if they had their tickets yet for London 2012 - as he wanted to be on the front row.
mt: 戴利说，他父亲无法接受他即将离世的事实，在他离世前最后的请求之一便是询问是否已经拿到了 2012 年伦敦奥运会的门票——因为他希望坐在第一排。

src: "I couldn't say to him 'you're not going to be around to be on the front row dad'," he said.
mt: “我没法对他说：‘爸，您不会有机会坐在前排了，’”他说。

src: "I was holding his hand as he stopped breathing and it wasn't until he'd actually stopped breathing and he was dead that I finally acknowledged he wasn't invincible," he said.
mt: “当他停止呼吸时，我握着他，直到他确实不再呼吸、确认已经离世，我才终于意识到他并非无所不能。”他说。

src: The following year Daley competed at the 2012 Olympics and won bronze.
mt: 次年，达利在 2012 年奥运会上夺得铜牌。

src: "I just knew that this is what I had dreamt of my whole life - to dive in front of a home crowd at an Olympic Games, there was no better feeling," he said.
mt: “我就知道这就是我梦寐以求的一刻——在全民欢呼的奥运赛场上完成一次跳水，这种感觉无以复加。”他说道。

src: It also inspired his first song choice - Proud by Heather Small - which had resonated with him in the build up to the Olympics and still gave him goosebumps.
mt: 这首歌也启发他选择了首支参赛曲目——Heather Small 的《Proud》。这首歌在奥运会筹备期间一直深深打动着他，至今仍让他起鸡皮疙瘩。

src: Desert Island Discs is on BBC Radio 4 on Sunday at 11:15 BST.
mt: 《荒岛八音》将于周日 BBC 第四台播出，时间为格林尼治标准时间 11:15。

src: Out-of-form Mickelson benched on Ryder Cup Saturday
mt: 状态不佳的米克森周六在莱德杯中被换下场

src: American Phil Mickelson will set a record on Sunday when he plays his 47th Ryder Cup match, but he will have to turn his form around to avoid it being an unhappy milestone.
mt: 美国高尔夫球手菲尔·米克尔森（Phil Mickelson）周日将参加他第 47 届莱德杯比赛，有望创造纪录，但他若想避免这一里程碑令人沮丧，就必须扭转当前的低迷状态。

src: Mickelson, playing in the biennial event for a record 12th time, was benched by captain Jim Furyk for Saturday's fourballs and foursomes.
mt: 米克森参加这项每两年举办一次的赛事，已是第 12 次刷新纪录，但他被队长吉姆·福瑞克安排坐在替补席上，未能在周六参加四人四球赛和四人两球赛。

src: Instead of being at the center of the action, as he so often has been for the United States, the five-times major winner split his day between being a cheerleader and working on his game on the range in the hope of rectifying what ails him.
mt: 与以往多次代表美国出战时身居核心不同，这位五次大满贯冠军将一天分为两部分：一方面为队友加油鼓劲，另一方面则在靶场苦练球技，以期纠正自身的问题。

src: Never the straightest of drivers even at the peak of his career, the 48-year-old is not an ideal fit for the tight Le Golf National course, where the long rough routinely punishes errant shots.
mt: 即便在职业生涯巅峰期，他从来都不是最精准的球手，而这位 48 岁的选手也并非勒·古尔夫·纳申纳尔短小赛道的理想人选，那里杂乱的长草区往往会惩罚那些偏离轨道的击球。

src: And if the course on its own is not daunting enough, Mickelson, in the ninth match on Sunday, faces accurate British Open champion Francesco Molinari, who has teamed up with rookie Tommy Fleetwood to win all four of their matches this week.
mt: 如果单看赛程已令人望而生畏，米克尔森在周日第九轮还将迎战英国公开赛冠军、精准度极高的弗朗切斯科·莫利纳里。莫利纳里已与新秀汤米·弗利特伍德组队，本周已赢得他们四场全部比赛。

src: If the Americans, four points down starting the 12 singles matches, get off to a hot start, Mickelson's match could prove absolutely crucial.
mt: 如果美国队在首轮四人单打比赛中以四比零落后，但能开局抢热，那么米克尔森的比赛就可能显得至关重要。

src: Furyk expressed confidence in his man, not that he could say much else.
mt: 富里克对他的人信心满满，除此之外也无话可说。

src: "He fully understood the role that he had today, gave me a pat on the back and put his arm around me and said he would be ready tomorrow," Furyk said.
mt: 福瑞克表示：“他完全理解自己今天所扮演的角色，拍了拍我的后背，揽住我的肩膀，并表示他明天会做好准备。”

src: "He's got a lot of confidence in himself.
mt: 他对自己信心十足。

src: He's a Hall of Famer and he's added so much to these teams in the past, and this week.
mt: 他是一位名人堂球员，过去及本周都为这些球队做出了巨大贡献。

src: I probably didn't envision him playing two matches.
mt: 我大概没料到他会连续打两场。

src: I envisioned more, but that's the way it worked out and that's the way we thought we had to go.
mt: 我本有更宏大的构想，但事情的发展便是如此，我们也认为这是不得不走的道路。

src: He wants to be out there, just like everyone else."
mt: 他想像其他人一样，身处其中。

src: Mickelson will pass Nick Faldo's record for the most Ryder Cup matches played on Sunday.
mt: 周日，米克森将追平尼克·法尔多保持的莱德杯参赛场次纪录。

src: It could mark the end of a Ryder Cup career that has never quite matched the heights of his individual record.
mt: 这或许标志着他的莱德杯生涯的终结，而该生涯的成就始终未能达到其个人最佳成绩的巅峰。

src: Mickelson has 18 wins, 20 losses and seven halves, though Furyk said his presence brought some intangibles to the team.
mt: 米克尔森取得了 18 胜 20 负以及七次半程冠军，不过富瑞克表示他的存在为团队带来了一些难以量化的价值。

src: "He's funny, he's sarcastic, witty, likes to poke fun at people, and he's a great guy to have in the team room," he explained.
mt: “他幽默风趣，言辞犀利，机智诙谐，喜欢拿人开玩笑，而且是个团队中不可或缺的好伙伴，”他解释道。

src: "I think the younger players had fun having a go at him, as well, this week, which was fun to see.
mt: 我也觉得年轻球员本周有机会挑战他，大家玩得很开心，这景象令人愉悦。

src: He provides a lot more than just play."
mt: 他提供的远不止是娱乐。

src: Europe captain Thomas Bjorn knows big lead can soon disappear
mt: 欧洲队长托马斯·比约恩深知领先优势可能随时消失。

src: Thomas Bjorn, the European captain, knows from experience that a sizeable lead heading into the last-day singles in the Ryder Cup can easily turn into an uncomfortable ride.
mt: 欧洲队长托马斯·比约恩深知，根据过往经验，莱德杯最后一天单打开赛前若优势明显，反而可能演变成一段艰难的旅程。

src: The Dane made his debut in the 1997 match at Valderrama, where a side captained by Seve Ballesteros held a five-point advantage over the Americans but only just got over the finishing line with their noses in front by the narrowest of margins, winning 14½-13½.
mt: 这位丹麦选手在 1997 年瓦尔德贝拉莫锦标赛上首次亮相，当时由西维·巴莱斯特罗斯率领的丹麦队以 14½-13½ 的微弱优势险胜美国队，尽管比赛过程中他们一度落后五杆。

src: "You keep reminding yourself that we had a big lead at Valderrama; we had a big lead at Brookline, where we lost, and at Valderrama, where we won, but only just," said Bjorn, pictured, after watching the Class of 2018 win 5-3 both on Friday and yesterday to lead 10-6 at Le Golf National.
mt: “你一直在提醒自己，我们在瓦尔德拉马曾占据巨大优势；我们在布鲁克莱恩也曾一度领先，但最终输掉了比赛；而在瓦尔德拉马，我们虽然赢了，但优势微弱。”画面中的比约恩在观看 2018 届选手于周五和昨天均以 5-3 获胜，从而在法国勒高尔夫国民球场以 10-6 领跑后说道。

src: So history will show me and everybody on that team that this is not over.
mt: 历史终将证明我和整个队伍，比赛尚未结束。

src: You go full bore tomorrow.
mt: 明天全力以赴。

src: Get out there and do all the right things.
mt: 走出去，把该做的事全都做好。

src: This is not over till you've got the points on the board.
mt: 只要分数还没上板，比赛就没有结束。

src: We have a goal, and that is to try to win this trophy, and that's where the focus stays.
mt: 我们的目标就是赢得这座奖杯，而我们的注意力也始终聚焦于此。

src: I've said all along, I focus on the 12 players that are in our side, but we are so well aware of what's standing across on the other side - the greatest players in the world."
mt: 我一直强调，我们的关注点在于队内的 12 名球员，但我们深知对手那边站着的是世界上最顶尖的球星。

src: Delighted how his players have performed on a tough golf course, Bjorn added: "I would never get ahead of myself in this.
mt: Bjorn 表示：“我对球队在艰难球道上的表现感到非常高兴，但我绝不会因此得意忘形。”

src: Tomorrow's a different beast.
mt: 明天是另一番景象。

src: Tomorrow is the individual performances that come forward, and that is a different thing to do.
mt: 明天是接下来的个人表演环节，那是需要另外处理的事情。

src: It's great to be out there with a partner when things are going good, but when you're out there individually, then you're tested to the full of your capacity as a golfer.
mt: 与搭档一同参赛时，若一切顺利，那确实令人愉悦；但若独自出战，则能充分考验你作为高尔夫球手的全部实力。

src: That's the message that you need to get across to players, is get the best out of yourself tomorrow.
mt: 你需要传达给球员的信息是：明天要全力以赴，发挥出最好的自己。

src: Now, you leave your partner behind and he has to go and get the best out of himself, as well."
mt: 现在，你要把搭档抛在脑后，让他去发挥自己最大的潜力。”

src: In contrast to Bjorn, opposite number Jim Furyk will be looking for his players to perform better individually than they did as partners, the exceptions being Jordan Spieth and Justin Thomas, who picked up three points out of four.
mt: 与比约恩不同，他的对手吉姆·福瑞克将期望其球员在个人表现上优于搭档配合时的成绩，乔丹·斯皮思和贾斯汀·托马斯除外，这两人四场比赛夺得了三分。

src: Furyk himself has been on both ends of those big last-day turnarounds, having been part of the winning team at Brookline before ending up a loser as Europe pulled off the "Miracle at Medinah."
mt: 富瑞克本人也曾经历过这两种截然不同的结局：他在布鲁克莱恩赛上属于获胜方，却在欧米塔在“米迪兰奇迹”中夺冠时沦为败者。

src: "I remember every damn word of it," he said in reply to being asked how Ben Crenshaw, the captain in 1999, had rallied his players heading into the last day.
mt: “我记性差，但每一个字都记得清清楚楚，”他回答说，回应的是人们问他，1999 年的队长本·克雷南在最后一天的比赛前是如何激励队员们的。

src: "We have 12 important matches tomorrow, but you'd like to get off to that fast start like you saw at Brookline, like you saw at Medinah.
mt: “我们明天有 12 场重要比赛，但你希望能像之前在布鲁克莱恩和麦迪纳那样迅速取得开门红。”

src: When that momentum gets going one way, it puts a lot of pressure on those middle matches.
mt: 一旦这种势头形成，会给中间的比赛带来巨大压力。

src: We set up our line-up accordingly and put the guys out in the fashion that we felt like, you know, we're trying to make some magic tomorrow."
mt: 因此，我们按照自己的想法组建了阵容，派出队员，因为我们希望明天能创造奇迹。

src: Thomas has been handed the task of trying to lead the fightback and faces Rory McIlroy in the top match, with Paul Casey, Justin Rose, Jon Rahm, Tommy Fleetwood and Ian Poulter the other Europeans in the top half of the order.
mt: 托马斯被委以重任，负责率领欧洲队发起反击，他将与罗里·麦克罗伊争夺头号位置，而保罗·凯西、贾斯汀·罗斯、约恩·拉姆、汤米·弗莱特伍德和伊恩·保尔特则是欧洲队上半区的其他选手。

src: "I went with this group of guys in this order because I think it covers all the way through," said Bjorn of his singles selections.
mt: “我认为这个单打阵容的选人顺序能够覆盖全程，”比约恩说道。

src: Germany's new warship postponed yet again
mt: 德国新战舰再度推迟

src: German Navy's newest frigate should have been commissioned in 2014 to replace ageing Cold War-era warships, but it won't be there until at least the next year due to faulty systems and snowballing cost, local media reported.
mt: 据当地媒体报道，德国海军最新一艘护卫舰本应于 2014 年服役以取代冷战时期的老旧战舰，但因系统故障和成本不断攀升，其交付时间将推迟至明年至少。

src: Commissioning of the "Rheinland-Pfalz," the lead ship of the brand new Baden-Wuerttemberg-class frigates, has now been postponed until the first half of 2019, according to Die Zeit newspaper citing a military spokesman.
mt: 据《时代》报援引军方发言人消息，巴登 - 符腾堡级护卫舰首舰“莱茵兰 - 普法尔茨”号的服役工作现已推迟至 2019 年上半年。

src: The vessel should have joined the Navy in 2014, but the troubling post-delivery issues plagued the fate of the ambitious project.
mt: 该舰本应于 2014 年加入海军，但交付后出现的一系列令人担忧的问题，致使这一雄心勃勃的项目屡遭挫折。

src: The four Baden-Wuerttemberg-class vessels the Navy ordered back in 2007 will come as replacement to the ageing Bremen-class frigates.
mt: 德国海军于 2007 年订购的四艘“ Baden-Württemberg"级舰艇将用于替换老旧的“不来梅”级护卫舰。

src: It is understood they will feature a powerful cannon, an array of anti-aircraft and anti-ship missiles as well as some stealth technologies, such as reduced radar, infrared and acoustic signatures.
mt: 据悉，该舰将配备威力强大的火炮、多种防空和反舰导弹，以及部分隐身技术，包括降低雷达、红外和声呐特征。

src: Other important features include longer maintenance periods - it should be possible to deploy the newest frigates for up to two years away from home ports.
mt: 其他重要特性包括更长的维护周期——新型护卫舰应能实现距母港长达两年的部署。

src: However, continuous delays mean that the cutting-edge warships - said to allow Germany to project power overseas - will already become outdated by the time they enter service, Die Zeit notes.
mt: 然而，《时代》周刊指出，持续不断的延误意味着这些号称能让德国在海外投射力量的尖端战舰，在服役之时将早已过时。

src: The ill-fated F125 frigate made headlines last year, when the German Navy officially refused to commission the vessel and returned it to Blohm & Voss shipyard in Hamburg.
mt: 去年，命运多舛的 F125 护卫舰引发媒体关注，德国海军正式拒绝将其投入服役，并将其返还至汉堡的布洛姆&福斯造船厂。

src: This was the first time the Navy has returned a ship to a shipbuilder after delivery.
mt: 这是海军首次将一艘已交付的舰船退回给造船厂。

src: Little was known about the reasons behind the return, but German media cited a number of crucial "software and hardware defects" that made the warship useless if deployed on a combat mission.
mt: 关于返航的原因鲜为人知，但德国媒体报道称，该战舰存在多项关键的“软硬件缺陷”，若投入作战任务将毫无用处。

src: Software deficiencies were particularly important as the Baden-Wuerttemberg-class vessels will be operated by a crew of some 120 sailors - just half of the manpower on older Bremen class frigates.
mt: 巴登 - 符腾堡级舰艇将由约 120 名船员操作，仅为旧型不来梅级护卫舰人员编制的一半，因此软件缺陷显得尤为关键。

src: Also, it emerged that the ship is dramatically overweight which reduces its performance and limits the Navy's ability to add future upgrades.
mt: 此外，该舰严重超重的问题也浮出水面，这不仅降低了其性能，还限制了海军未来进行升级的能力。

src: The 7,000-ton "Rheinland-Pfalz" is believed to be twice as heavy as similar-class ships used by the Germans in the Second World War.
mt: 据悉，7000吨的“莱茵兰 - 普法尔茨”号重量是二战期间德国同类舰艇的两倍。

src: Aside from faulty hardware, the price tag of the entire project - including the training of the crew - is also becoming an issue.
mt: 除硬件故障外，整个项目（包括船员培训）的造价也正日益成为问题。

src: It is said to have reached staggering €3.1billion ($3.6bn) - up from initial €2.2 billion.
mt: 据悉，金额已飙升至惊人的 31 亿欧元（合 36 亿美元），较最初的 22 亿欧元有所增长。

src: Problems gripping the newest frigates become especially of importance in light of recent warnings that Germany's naval power is shrinking.
mt: 随着近期警告称德国海军力量正在萎缩，新护卫舰在抓握方面出现的问题显得尤为关键。

src: Earlier this year, Hans-Peter Bartels, chief of the German parliament's defense committee, acknowledged the Navy is actually "running out of deployment-capable ships."
mt: 今年早些时候，德国议会国防委员会主席汉斯 - 彼得·巴特尔承认，德国海军实际上“已无力维持舰艇的部署能力”。

src: The official said the issue has snowballed over time, because old ships were decommissioned but no replacement vessels were provided.
mt: 官员表示，这一问题随时间推移日益严重，原因在于旧船退役后并未补充新船。

src: He lamented that none of the of the Baden-Wuerttemberg-class frigates were able to join the Navy.
mt: 他 lamented 称，巴登 - 符腾堡级护卫舰无一能加入海军。

src: National Trust eavesdrops on secret life of bats
mt: 国家信托组织窃听蝙蝠的隐秘生活

src: New research being carried out at an estate in the Scottish Highlands aims to reveal how bats use the landscape in their hunt for food.
mt: 在新近于苏格兰高地的一处庄园开展的研究，旨在揭示蝙蝠如何在觅食过程中利用地形。

src: It is hoped the findings will shed new light on the behaviour of the unique flying mammals and help guide future conservation activities.
mt: 希望这些发现能为这种独特的飞行哺乳动物的行为提供新见解，并为今后的保育工作提供指导。

src: The study by scientists at the National Trust for Scotland will follow common and soprano pipistrelles as well as brown long-eared and Daubenton bats at Inverewe Gardens in Wester Ross.
mt: 苏格兰国家信托的科学家将追踪苏格兰西部罗斯区因韦花园中的普通蝙蝠、 Soprano 蝠鼠（ soprano pipistrelles）、棕耳蝠及大耳蝠。

src: Special recorders will be placed at key locations around the property to track bat activities throughout the season.
mt: 将在物业周边关键位置部署专用记录设备，以全程监测蝙蝠的活动情况。

src: NHS staff and volunteers will also carry out mobile surveys using hand-held detectors.
mt: 英国国家医疗服务体系（NHS）的工作人员和志愿者还将使用手持探测器开展移动式调查。

src: Expert sound analysis of all recordings will ascertain the frequency of the bat calls and which species are doing what.
mt: 所有录音将接受专家声学分析，以确定蝙蝠叫声的频率及其所属物种及其行为。

src: A habitat map and report will then be produced to create a detailed landscape-scale picture of their behaviour.
mt: 随后，将编制栖息地地图和报告，以详细呈现其在景观尺度上的行为模式。

src: Rob Dewar, nature conservation adviser for NTS, hopes the results will reveal which areas of habitat are most important to the bats and how they are used by each of the species.
mt: 罗布·迪沃是苏格兰自然遗产局（NTS）的自然保护顾问，他希望调查结果能够揭示哪些栖息地对蝙蝠最为重要，以及每种蝙蝠如何利用这些区域。

src: This information will help determine the benefits of habitat management work such as meadow creation and how best to maintain woodlands for bats and other associated species.
mt: 这些信息将有助于确定草甸营造等生境管理工作的效益，以及如何最佳地维护用于保护蝙蝠及其他相关物种的林地。

src: Bat populations in Scotland and across the UK have declined considerably over the past century.
mt: 过去一个世纪以来，苏格兰及全英国的蝙蝠种群数量大幅减少。

src: They are under threat from building and development work that affects roosts and loss of habitat.
mt: 它们正面临因建筑和开发工程而破坏栖息地及巢址的威胁。

src: Wind turbines and lighting can also pose a risk, as can flypapers and some chemical treatments of building materials, as well as attacks by pet cats.
mt: 风力涡轮机和照明设施也可能构成风险，飞纸以及某些建筑材料中的化学处理剂亦属此类，此外还有家猫的袭击。

src: Bats are not actually blind.
mt: 蝙蝠其实并非瞎子。

src: However, due to their nocturnal hunting habits their ears are more useful than their eyes when it comes to catching prey.
mt: 然而，由于它们夜间捕猎的习性，耳朵在捕捉猎物时的作用比眼睛更为重要。

src: They use a sophisticated echo-location technique to pinpoint bugs and obstacles in their flight path.
mt: 它们使用精密的声纳定位技术来精准探测飞行路径上的昆虫和障碍物。

src: The NTS, which is responsible for the care of more than 270 historical buildings, 38 important gardens and 76,000 hectares of land around the country, takes bats very seriously.
mt: 国家古迹信托（NTS）负责照料全国超过 270 座历史建筑、38 座重要花园以及约 7.6 万公顷土地，其对蝙蝠的保护工作尤为重视。

src: It has ten trained experts, who regularly carry out surveys, roost inspections and sometimes rescues.
mt: 它拥有十名受过训练的专家，定期开展调查、巢穴检查，有时还会进行救援行动。

src: The organisation has even set up Scotland's first and only dedicated bat reserve at Threave estate in Dumfries and Galloway, which is home to eight of Scotland's ten bat species.
mt: 该组织甚至在苏格兰邓弗里斯和加洛韦的瑟雷庄园设立了苏格兰首个也是唯一的蝙蝠专用保护区，该地栖息着苏格兰十大蝙蝠物种中的八种。

src: Estate manager David Thompson says the estate is the ideal territory for them.
mt: 庄园管家大卫·汤普森表示，该庄园是他们的理想领地。

src: "Here at Threave we have a great area for bats," he said.
mt: “在我们特雷韦，这里非常适合蝙蝠栖息，”他说。

src: "We've got the old buildings, lots of veteran trees and all the good habitat.
mt: 我们拥有古老的建筑、众多古树以及丰富的栖息地。

src: But there is much about bats that is still unknown, so the work we do here and at other properties will help us understand more about what they need to thrive."
mt: 但关于蝙蝠，我们仍有许多未知之处，因此我们在此处及其他场所开展的工作，将有助于我们更深入地了解它们生存所需的条件。"

src: He stresses the importance of checking for bats before carrying out maintenance within properties as it is possible unwitting destruction of a single maternity roost could kill up to 400 females and young, possibly wiping out an entire local population.
mt: 他强调在进行建筑物维护前必须进行蝙蝠检查，因为无意中摧毁一处 maternity 产巢可能导致多达 400 只雌性蝙蝠及幼崽死亡，甚至可能使整个当地种群灭绝。

src: Bats are protected and it is illegal to kill, harass or disturb them or destroy their roosts.
mt: 蝙蝠受法律保护，杀害、骚扰或干扰蝙蝠，或破坏其栖息地均属违法行为。

src: Elisabeth Ferrell, Scottish officer for the Bat Conservation Trust, has encouraged the public to pitch in to help.
mt: 苏格兰野生动物保护信托基金的官员伊丽莎白·费尔尔呼吁公众踊跃参与，伸出援手。

src: She said: "We still have a lot to learn about our bats and for many of our species we just don't know how their populations are faring."
mt: 她表示：“我们仍对蝙蝠了解甚少，对于许多物种，我们甚至不知道其种群状况如何。”

src: Ronaldo dismisses rape claims as lawyers set to sue German magazine
mt: 罗纳尔多斥责强奸指控，律师正准备对德国杂志提起诉讼

src: Cristiano Ronaldo has branded rape claims against him as "fake news," saying that people "want to promote themselves" by using his name.
mt: C 罗将针对他的强奸指控称为“假新闻”，并表示人们希望借他的名字来博取关注。

src: His lawyers are set to sue German news magazine Der Spiegel, which published the allegations.
mt: 他的律师正准备起诉德国新闻杂志《明镜周刊》，该杂志发表了相关指控。

src: The Portugal and Juventus forward has been accused of raping an American woman, named as Kathryn Mayorga, in a Las Vegas hotel room in 2009.
mt: 葡萄牙和尤文图斯的前锋被指控于 2009 年在拉斯维加斯的一家酒店房间内强奸一名美国女子凯瑟琳·马约尔加。

src: He is alleged to have then paid her $375,000 to keep quiet about the incident, Der Spiegel reported on Friday.
mt: 据《明镜》周刊周五报道，他被指控随后支付了该女子 37.5 万美元以封口。

src: Speaking in an Instagram Live video to his 142 million followers hours after the claims were reported, Ronaldo, 33, slammed the reports as "fake news."
mt: 在相关报道曝光数小时后，33 岁的罗纳尔多在一段面向 1.42 亿粉丝的 Instagram 直播视频中，痛斥这些报道为“假新闻”。

src: "No, no, no, no , no.
mt: 不，不，不，不，不。

src: What they said today, fake news," the five-time Ballon d'Or winner says into the camera.
mt: “今天他们说的是假新闻，”这位五届金球奖得主对着镜头说道。

src: "They want to promote themselves by using my name.
mt: 他们想借我的名字来炒作自己。

src: It's normal.
mt: 这很正常。

src: They want to be famous to say my name, but it is part of the job.
mt: 他们想出名好让我出名，但这正是工作的一部分。

src: I am a happy man and all good," the player added, smiling.
mt: “我很快乐，一切安好，”这位球员笑着补充道。

src: Ronaldo's lawyers are preparing to sue Der Spiegel over the allegations, which they have called "an inadmissible reporting of suspicions in the area of privacy," according to Reuters.
mt: 据路透社报道，罗纳尔多的律师正计划起诉《明镜》周刊，称其报道属于“在隐私领域不当披露嫌疑”。

src: Lawyer Christian Schertz said the player would seek compensation for "moral damages in an amount corresponding to the gravity of the infringement, which is probably one of the most serious violations of personal rights in recent years."
mt: 律师克里斯蒂安·舍尔茨表示，该球员将寻求赔偿，金额应与其所遭受的道德损害相当，而此次侵权行为的严重程度或许为近年来对个人权利最严重的侵害之一。

src: The alleged incident is said to have taken place in June 2009 at a suite at the Palms Hotel and Casino in Las Vegas.
mt: 据称，该事件发生于2009年6月，地点位于拉斯维加斯的棕榈酒店和赌场的一处套房内。

src: After meeting in a nightclub, Ronaldo and Mayorga reportedly went back to the player's room, where he allegedly anally raped her, according to papers filed at Clark County District Court in Nevada.
mt: 据报道，在与罗纳尔多在夜店会面后，两人返回了球员的房间，内华达州克拉克县地方法院提交的文件指控其对她实施了肛交强奸。

src: Mayorga claims Ronaldo fell to his knees after the alleged incident and told her he was "99 percent" a "good guy" let down by the "one percent."
mt: 马约尔加声称，在疑似事件发生后，罗纳尔多双膝跪地，告诉她自己"99%"是个“好人”，只是被那“1%"给拖累了。

src: The documents claim that Ronaldo confirmed the pair had sex, but that it was consensual.
mt: 文件称，罗纳尔多承认两人发生过性关系，但强调是双方自愿的。

src: Mayorga also claims she went to the police and had photographs taken of her injuries at a hospital, but later agreed to an out-of-court settlement because she felt "terrified of retaliation" and was worried about "being publicly humiliated."
mt: 马约尔加还声称她曾报警并在医院拍摄了伤情照片，但后来因“害怕遭到报复”和担心“遭受公开羞辱”而同意达成庭外和解。

src: The 34-year-old says she is now seeking to overturn the settlement as she continues to be traumatized by the alleged incident.
mt: 这位 34 岁的女子表示，她正寻求推翻和解协议，因为该事件对她造成的创伤至今仍未愈合。

src: Ronaldo was on the verge of joining Real Madrid from Manchester United at the time of the alleged assault, and this summer moved to Italian giants Juve in a €100 million deal.
mt: 当时，罗纳尔多正准备从曼联转会皇家马德里，而今年夏天他以一亿欧元的转会费加盟了意大利豪门尤文图斯。

src: Brexit: UK 'would forever regret' losing carmakers
mt: 脱欧：英国“将永远 regret 失去汽车制造商”

src: The UK "would regret it forever" if it lost its status as a world leader in car manufacturing after Brexit, Business Secretary Greg Clark has said.
mt: 英国商务大臣格雷格·克拉克表示，若脱欧后英国失去世界汽车制造强国地位，将“永远感到遗憾”。

src: He added it was "concerning" that Toyota UK had told the BBC that if Britain left the EU without a deal it would temporarily halt production at its factory in Burnaston, near Derby.
mt: 他还表示，丰田英国方面曾向 BBC 表示，若英国脱离欧盟且未达成协议，将暂时停摆其位于德比附近伯恩斯顿的工厂生产，此事“令人担忧”。

src: "We need a deal," Mr Clark said.
mt: “我们需要达成协议。”克拉克先生说。

src: The Japanese carmaker said the impact of border delays in the event of a no-deal Brexit could cost jobs.
mt: 日本车企表示，若发生无协议脱欧，边境延误可能导致就业岗位流失。

src: The Burnaston plant - which makes Toyota's Auris and Avensis - produced nearly 150,000 cars last year of which 90% were exported to the rest of the European Union.
mt: 伯恩aston工厂负责生产丰田 Auris 和 Avensis 车型，去年产量接近 15 万辆，其中 90% 出口至欧盟其他国家。

src: "My view is that if Britain crashes out of the EU at the end of March we will see production stops in our factory," said Marvin Cooke, Toyota's managing director at Burnaston.
mt: “如果英国在三月底退出欧盟，我们工厂的停产将不可避免，”丰田伯恩aston工厂总经理马文·库克表示。

src: Other UK car manufacturers have raised fears about leaving the EU without agreement on how cross-border trade will function, including Honda, BMW and Jaguar Land Rover.
mt: 其他英国汽车制造商，包括本田、宝马和捷豹路虎，表达了对在无协议情况下脱离欧盟后跨境贸易如何运作的担忧。

src: BMW, for example, says it will close its Mini plant in Oxford for a month following Brexit.
mt: 例如，宝马公司表示，受脱欧影响，其位于牛津的迷你汽车工厂将关闭一个月。

src: The main concerns relate to what carmakers say are supply chain risks in the event of a no-deal Brexit.
mt: 主要担忧在于，车企称若出现无协议脱欧，供应链将面临风险。

src: Toyota's production line is run on a "just-in-time" basis, with parts arriving every 37 minutes from suppliers in both the UK and the EU for cars made to order.
mt: 丰田的生产线采用“准时制”运作，零部件由英国和欧盟的供应商每 37 分钟送达一次，以满足定制化汽车的生产需求。

src: If the UK leaves the EU without a deal on 29 March, there could be disruption at the border which the industry says could lead to delays and shortages of parts.
mt: 如果英国在 3 月 29 日脱欧时未能与欧盟达成协议，边境可能会出现混乱，行业指出这可能导致零部件延误和短缺。

src: It would be impossible for Toyota to hold more than a day's worth of inventory at its Derbyshire plant, the company said, and so production would be stopped.
mt: 丰田公司表示，其在德比郡工厂的库存不可能超过一天的用量，因此将停止生产。

src: Mr Clark said Theresa May's Chequers plan for future relations with the EU is "precisely calibrated to avoid those checks at the border."
mt: 克拉克表示，特雷莎·梅的切克斯计划旨在精准规避边境检查。

src: "We need to have a deal. We want to have the best deal that will allow as I say not just the success at present to be enjoyed but for us to grasp this opportunity," he told BBC Radio 4's Today programme.
mt: “我们需要达成一项协议。我们希望达成一项最佳协议，正如我所说的，不仅能让当下的成功得以延续，还能让我们抓住这一机遇。”他在 BBC 电台 4 台的《今日》节目中说道。

src: "The evidence from not just Toyota but other manufacturers is that we need to absolutely be able to continue what has been a highly successful set of supply chains."
mt: 不仅来自丰田，其他制造商的证据也表明，我们必须能够继续保持这一套高度成功的供应链体系。

src: Toyota was unable to say how long production would be stopped, but in the longer term, warned that added costs would reduce the plant's competitiveness and eventually cost jobs.
mt: 丰田方面无法说明停产将持续多久，但警告称，长期来看，增加的运营成本将削弱该工厂的竞争力，最终导致裁员。

src: Peter Tsouvallaris, who has worked at Burnaston for 24 years and is the Unite union convenor at the plant, said his members are increasingly concerned: "In my experience once these jobs go they never come back.
mt: 彼得·苏瓦拉斯在伯恩阿斯顿工厂工作了 24 年，是该厂 Unite 工会的主席。他表示，他的成员们越来越担忧：“根据我的经验，一旦这些岗位消失，就再也回不来了。”

src: A government spokesperson said: "We have put forward a precise and credible plan for our future relationship with the EU."
mt: 一位政府发言人表示：“我们已经为与欧盟的未来关系制定了精准且可信的计划。”

src: Trump meeting with Rosenstein may be delayed again, says White House
mt: 白宫称，特朗普与罗斯林德的会议或再次推迟。

src: Donald Trump's high-stakes meeting with deputy attorney general Rod Rosenstein could be "pushed back another week" as the fight over supreme court nominee Brett Kavanaugh continues, the White House said on Sunday.
mt: 随着最高法院大法官提名人选 Brett Kavanaugh 的争议持续发酵，白宫周日表示，唐纳德·特朗普与代理司法部长罗德·罗斯林的高层会晤可能推迟一周。

src: Rosenstein oversees the work of special counsel Robert Mueller, who is investigating Russian election interference, links between Trump aides and Russia and potential obstruction of justice by the president.
mt: 罗斯宁负责监督特别检察官罗伯特·穆勒的工作，穆勒正在调查俄罗斯干预美国选举、特朗普团队与俄罗斯之间的关联，以及总统涉嫌妨碍司法公正的行为。

src: Whether or not Trump will fire the deputy attorney general, and thereby endanger Mueller's independence, has fuelled Washington gossip for months.
mt: 特朗普是否会解雇副总检察长并进而危及穆勒的独立性，这一问题已引发华盛顿数月来的流言蜚语。

src: Earlier this month, the New York Times reported that Rosenstein discussed wearing a wire to record conversations with Trump and the possibility of removing the president via the 25th amendment.
mt: 本月早些时候，纽约时报报道，罗斯汀讨论了在特朗普身边安装窃听装置以记录谈话内容，以及通过第二十五修正案罢免总统的可能性。

src: Rosenstein denied the report.
mt: 罗斯坦否认了该报道。

src: But last Monday he went to the White House, amid reports he was about to resign.
mt: 但上周日，在外界传他将辞职之际，他前往了白宫。

src: Instead, a meeting with Trump, who was then at the United Nations in New York, was announced for Thursday.
mt: 相反，原定周四与当时正在纽约出席联合国大会的特朗普会面。

src: Trump said he would "prefer not" to fire Rosenstein but then the meeting was delayed to avoid a clash with the Senate judiciary committee hearing in which Kavanaugh and one of the women who have accused him of sexual misconduct, Dr Christine Blasey Ford, both testified.
mt: 特朗普表示“更倾向于不”解雇罗斯林，但随后会议被推迟，以避免与参议院司法委员会的听证会发生冲突。在该听证会上，卡瓦诺与一位指控他性不当行为的女性——克里斯汀·布雷西·福德博士——均出庭作证。

src: On Friday, Trump ordered a one-week FBI investigation of claims against Kavanaugh, further delaying a full Senate vote.
mt: 周五，特朗普下令对针对卡瓦诺的指控开展为期一周的 FBI 调查，进一步推迟了参议院的全票表决。

src: Trump's press secretary, Sarah Sanders, appeared on Fox News Sunday.
mt: 特朗普的发言人萨莎·桑德斯出现在福克斯新闻的《周日早间节目》中。

src: Asked about the Rosenstein meeting, she said: "A date for that hasn't been set, it could be this week, I could see that pushing back another week given all of the other things that are going on with the supreme court.
mt: 谈及罗斯滕伯格会晤一事，她表示：“目前尚未确定具体日期，本周可能会举行，但考虑到最高法院的其他事务，我也可能将其推迟到下周。”

src: But we'll see and I always like to keep the press updated."
mt: “但我们拭目以待，我向来乐于向媒体更新消息。”

src: Some reporters would contest that assertion: Sanders has not held a White House press briefing since 10 September.
mt: 部分记者对此提出异议：桑德斯自 9 月 10 日以来未曾在白宫举行记者会。

src: Host Chris Wallace asked why.
mt: 主持人克里斯·华莱士问起了原因。

src: Sanders said the scarcity of briefings was not due to a distaste for TV reporters "grandstanding," although she said: "I won't disagree with the fact that they grandstand."
mt: 桑德斯表示，简报会稀少并非因为她厌恶电视记者的“哗众取宠”，尽管她也说道：“我也不会否认他们确实在哗众取宠。”

src: She then suggested direct contact between Trump and the press will increase.
mt: 随后她建议，特朗普与媒体的直接接触将会增加。

src: "The president does more Q&A sessions than any president has prior to him," she said, adding without citing evidence: "We've looked at those numbers."
mt: “她称，这位总统主持的问答环节数量前所未有”，她补充道，但未提供证据：“我们查看过这些数据。”

src: Briefings will still happen, Sanders said, but "if the press has the chance to ask the president of the United States questions directly, that's infinitely better than talking to me.
mt: 布林肯表示简报会仍将继续，但“如果媒体有机会直接向美国总统提问，那比我接受采访要好得多。”

src: We try to do that a lot and you've seen us do that a lot over the last few weeks and that's going to take the place of a press briefing when you can talk to the president of the United States."
mt: 我们一直在努力这样做，过去几周你们也看到了我们频繁地这样做，这将取代传统的记者简报会，让你们有机会直接与美国总统对话。

src: Trump regularly takes questions when leaving the White House or participating in open sessions or press conferences with visiting dignitaries.
mt: 特朗普在离开白宫或参与与来访贵宾的公开会议或记者招待会时，通常会现场回答提问。

src: Solo press conferences are rare.
mt: 单独举行的新闻发布会十分罕见。

src: In New York this week the president perhaps demonstrated why, making a freewheeling and at times bizarre appearance before gathered reporters.
mt: 本周在纽约，总统的即兴且时而离奇的演讲或许揭示了其中的缘由。

src: Health secretary writes to EU workers at NHS Scotland over Brexit fears
mt: 英格蘭衛生部長致函蘇格蘭 NHS 的歐盟員工，表達對脫歐後就業擔憂的關注

src: The Health Secretary has written to EU staff working in Scotland's NHS to express the country's gratitude and wish for them to stay on post-Brexit.
mt: 英格蘭衛生部長致函在蘇格蘭國家醫療體系工作的歐盟員工，以表達蘇格蘭的謝意，並希望他們在脫歐後繼續留任。

src: Jeane Freeman MSP sent a letter with less than six months to go until the UK withdraws from the EU.
mt: 英国退出欧盟还有不到六个月的时间时，苏格兰议员简·弗里曼（Jeane Freeman）发了一封信。

src: The Scottish Government has already committed to meet the cost of settled status applications for EU citizens working in its devolved public services.
